H2O.description: H2O is a fast and secure HTTP/2 server written in C by Kazuho Oku.
H2O.history: H2O.ai was founded in 2012 by Sri Ambati in Mountain View, California, as an open-source machine learning platform for big data. It raised significant VC funding including from GE Ventures and Nvidia. The company is known for its AutoML capabilities, Driverless AI, and Wave UI framework. H2O.ai has remained independent and is widely used by enterprises for ML model training and deployment.
H2O.summary_abuse: H2O.ai is a machine learning platform with no abuse signal. It is foundational ML infrastructure used by data scientists and enterprises, with no consumer-facing surface that could be exploited for fraud or spam.

h5ai.description: h5ai is a modern HTTP web server index for Apache httpd, lighttpd, and nginx.
h5ai.history: h5ai is an open-source HTTP web server directory index created by Lars Jung, providing a modern HTML5 file browser for Apache, lighttpd, and nginx; maintained as an independent project since around 2011.
h5ai.summary_abuse: h5ai is a self-hosted directory listing tool with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

Haddock.description: Haddock is a tool for automatically generating documentation from annotated Haskell source code.
Haddock.history: Haddock is the standard documentation generation tool for Haskell, first developed by Simon Marlow around 2002 and now maintained by the Haskell Foundation. It is an integral part of the Haskell build ecosystem (Cabal/Stack) and generates API documentation from annotated Haskell source.
Haddock.summary_abuse: Haddock is a Haskell documentation tool with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse patterns. It is foundational tooling within the Haskell ecosystem.

Haku.description: Haku is a technology platform for endurance races that supports organizations in growing participation, increasing registrations, and improving event experiences.
Haku.history: Haku is a SaaS platform for endurance sports event registration and management, based in the US. It serves race organizers with registration, timing, and participant management tools. Founded in the mid-2010s as a niche competitor to platforms like RunSignUp and Race Roster.
Haku.summary_abuse: Haku is a niche sports event registration platform. No documented abuse patterns. As a payment-processing platform for event registrations, standard PCI compliance expectations apply. Small vendor with no public security program.

Halo.description: Halo is a powerful, user-friendly open-source website building tool that supports dynamic themes, real-time editing, and multilingual setups.
Halo.history: Halo is an open-source CMS and blogging platform developed in Java, originating from China and first published on GitHub around 2019. It supports dynamic themes, plugins, and multilingual setups. The project has gained a significant Chinese-language user base as a self-hosted blogging alternative.
Halo.summary_abuse: Halo is a self-hosted open-source CMS. No documented abuse patterns. As with any self-hosted publishing platform, the abuse risk is primarily from users deploying it for malicious purposes, but the software itself has no inherent abuse vectors. No notable CVEs documented.

Haloscan.description: Haloscan is a tool that enables blogs to incorporate trackbacks and comments, allowing users to engage with blog content through discussions and feedback.
Haloscan.history: Haloscan was an early blog commenting and trackback service popular in the mid-2000s blogging era. It offered free comment hosting for blogs. The service was shut down in January 2009 after being acquired by JS-Kit (later Echo). Users were migrated to Echo Comments before that service was also eventually discontinued.
Haloscan.summary_abuse: Haloscan was a legacy blog commenting service shut down in 2009. No longer operational. Historically it was used for legitimate blog discussions with no notable abuse patterns documented. Defunct service.

Hamechio.description: Hamechio is a web application framework.
Hamechio.history: Hamechio is a small web application framework with minimal public documentation or community presence. First appeared around 2020. Very limited adoption or activity observed.
Hamechio.summary_abuse: Hamechio is an obscure web framework with no documented abuse patterns and negligible adoption. No consumer-facing surface.

Hammer.js.description: Hammer.js is a JavaScript library for adding touch gestures like swipe, pinch, and rotate to web applications.
Hammer.js.history: Hammer.js is a JavaScript multi-touch gesture library created by Jorik Tangelder and first published around 2012. It became widely used for enabling swipe, pinch, rotate, and tap gestures on web applications. The project reached version 2.0 but has since fallen into low-maintenance mode, with the maintainer team noting volunteer-only development and very limited activity in recent years.
Hammer.js.summary_abuse: Hammer.js is a client-side gesture library with no server component, no data collection, and no abuse surface. It has no meaningful abuse profile.

Handlebars.description: Handlebars is a JavaScript library used to create reusable webpage templates.
Handlebars.history: Handlebars.js is a JavaScript templating language that extends Mustache templates, created by Yehuda Katz and first released around 2011. It became popular through Ember.js adoption and widespread server-side templating use. The project moved to the handlebars-lang GitHub organization and continues to receive occasional maintenance updates, with the last commit activity noted in early 2026.
Handlebars.summary_abuse: Handlebars.js is a client-side and server-side templating library with no network component or user-facing surface. It has no known abuse profile and presents negligible risk.

Handsontable.description: Handsontable is a JavaScript component that combines data grid features with spreadsheet-like UX.
Handsontable.history: Handsontable is a JavaScript data grid library first released in 2012 by Handsoncode, a Polish company. Initially MIT-licensed, the project switched to a proprietary/commercial license model in 2019 (version 7+), causing significant community controversy. A free non-commercial license remains available. The company continues active development with React, Angular, and Vue wrappers.
Handsontable.summary_abuse: Handsontable is a client-side JavaScript data grid component. No documented abuse patterns. Its license change from MIT to proprietary in 2019 caused controversy but is not an abuse concern. No security incidents documented.

Handtalk.description: Handtalk is an accessiblity plug-in which uses sign language to make sites accessible.
Handtalk.history: Handtalk is a Brazilian accessibility technology company founded in 2012, offering an AI-powered sign language translation plugin for websites. It primarily targets LIBRAS (Brazilian Sign Language) accessibility compliance. The company has received investment funding and serves Brazilian government and enterprise clients seeking web accessibility compliance.
Handtalk.summary_abuse: Handtalk is an accessibility plugin provider focused on sign language translation. No documented abuse patterns. As a third-party script loaded on websites, it has access to page content, which is standard for accessibility plugins. No known security incidents.

Hanko.description: Hanko is a passwordless authentication solution offering secure login integration.
Hanko.history: Hanko is a passwordless authentication platform founded around 2021 by a German team, offering passkey-based and WebAuthn authentication as both an open-source project (Apache 2.0) and a managed cloud service. It has gained traction in the developer community as passkeys adoption grew following FIDO2 standardization.
Hanko.summary_abuse: Hanko is an authentication infrastructure provider. Authentication systems can be targeted for credential attacks but as an auth provider rather than a consumer-facing service, abuse risk is limited to its API endpoints. No documented misuse or security incidents. German company with GDPR focus.

Hansel.description: Hansel is a B2B enterprise software that deploys real-time Nudges to drive feature adoption and address user drop-offs, at scale.
Hansel.history: Hansel is an Indian B2B SaaS company providing in-app product adoption and user onboarding tools (nudges, tooltips, walkthroughs) for mobile and web apps. Founded around 2015 in India, it targets enterprise customers in the product-led growth space, competing with Appcues and WalkMe.
Hansel.summary_abuse: Hansel is a product adoption SaaS that injects UI overlays into host applications. As a third-party script with DOM access, users should review data collection practices. No documented abuse patterns or security incidents. Small Indian startup without a formal security program.

hantana.description: Hantana provides user behavior analysis tools like heatmaps, surveys, and feedback widgets to enhance website user experience and customer satisfaction.
hantana.history: hantana is a user behavior analytics platform offering heatmaps, surveys, and feedback widgets to help improve website UX; no founding year or notable ownership history found in public records.
hantana.summary_abuse: hantana is a user behavior analytics tool with no consumer-facing content hosting surface. No documented abuse patterns have been reported.

Hapana.description: Hapana is a gym and fitness studio management platform designed to streamline operations.
Hapana.history: Hapana is an Australian SaaS platform for fitness studio and gym management, providing booking, membership, payment, and operations software. Founded around 2014, it serves boutique fitness brands and fitness franchises. The platform was previously known as Clubworx before rebranding to Hapana.
Hapana.summary_abuse: Hapana is a fitness business management platform. No documented abuse patterns. Handles payment and member data for fitness studios, so standard PCI and data protection concerns apply. Small Australian vendor without a formal security program.

Happy Meal Prep.description: Happy Meal Prep is a food order management system tailored for the food industry.
Happy Meal Prep.history: Happy Meal Prep is a food order management system for the meal prep and food delivery industry; limited public history available.
Happy Meal Prep.summary_abuse: Happy Meal Prep is a niche food order management platform with no notable abuse signal. It manages meal prep business operations and does not offer public content hosting.

Happy Returns.description: Happy Returns is a return software and reverse logistics company, provides a packaging-free, in-person way for customers to return an online purchase for an immediate refund.
Happy Returns.history: Happy Returns was founded in 2015 to offer packaging-free, in-person return drop-off points for e-commerce shoppers. PayPal acquired Happy Returns in 2021. UPS then acquired it from PayPal in 2023. The service operates a network of Return Bar locations across the US and provides returns software for e-commerce merchants.
Happy Returns.summary_abuse: Happy Returns is a reverse logistics and returns management platform backed by UPS. No documented abuse patterns. As a consumer-facing returns portal and merchant software, fraud risks are typical for retail returns (e.g., return fraud) but those are merchant concerns rather than platform abuse. Standard compliance under UPS ownership.

Happy Talk.description: HappyTalk is a customer consultation tool developed in Korea that automates interactions to support business communication.
Happy Talk.history: HappyTalk is a South Korean SaaS platform providing automated customer consultation and chatbot tools for business communication, primarily serving the Korean market. Founded in the mid-2010s, it offers live chat, chatbot automation, and CRM integrations for e-commerce and enterprise customers in Korea.
Happy Talk.summary_abuse: HappyTalk is a Korean customer communication SaaS. No documented abuse patterns for Western markets. Primarily serves the Korean market. As a chat widget provider, it has access to site visitor data, which is standard for live chat tools. No formal security program documented.

HappyFox Helpdesk.description: HappyFox is a help desk ticketing system that is hosted on cloud, supporting multiple customer support channels like email, voice and live chat.
HappyFox Helpdesk.history: HappyFox was founded in August 2011 by Shalin Jain and is operated by Tenmiles Technologies, a company also behind the earlier Help Desk Pilot product. It is bootstrapped and has grown to serve over 12,000 customers in 120 countries without raising venture capital. The company is headquartered in Irvine, California.
HappyFox Helpdesk.summary_abuse: HappyFox is a B2B helpdesk SaaS with no notable abuse profile. It serves business customers through authenticated accounts with no anonymous or consumer-facing sign-up surface that would attract abusers. No public incidents of abuse-related use have been documented.

HappyFox Live Chat.description: HappyFox is a help desk ticketing system that is hosted on cloud, supporting multiple customer support channels like email, voice and live chat.
HappyFox Live Chat.history: HappyFox Live Chat is a live chat product from HappyFox Inc., founded in 2011 by Shalin Jain and operated by Tenmiles Technologies. It extends the company's help desk offering into real-time customer chat. HappyFox is bootstrapped and independently operated out of Irvine, California.
HappyFox Live Chat.summary_abuse: HappyFox Live Chat is a B2B live chat widget embedded in business websites. The widget itself could theoretically be used to contact businesses with spam or social engineering, but this is a minor and well-understood risk in the category. No specific abuse incidents associated with HappyFox Live Chat have been publicly documented.

HappySync.description: HappySync is a Korean-developed tool designed for marketing automation and conversion optimization.
HappySync.history: HappySync is a Korean marketing automation and conversion optimization tool; limited public history available.
HappySync.summary_abuse: HappySync is a Korean marketing automation tool with no notable abuse signal documented. It embeds on websites for conversion tracking purposes.

Haptik.description: Haptik is an Indian enterprise conversational AI platform founded in August 2013, and acquired by Reliance Industries Limited in 2019.
Haptik.history: Haptik was founded in August 2013 in Mumbai, India as a conversational AI and chatbot platform. It raised significant VC funding before being acquired by Reliance Industries Limited (Jio Platforms) in 2019 for approximately $100 million, making it a subsidiary of one of India's largest conglomerates. Haptik now focuses on enterprise AI assistant deployments for large Indian and international enterprises.
Haptik.summary_abuse: Haptik is an enterprise conversational AI platform backed by Reliance Industries. No documented abuse patterns. As a chatbot/AI assistant provider, it processes user conversations which raises standard data privacy considerations. Primarily serves enterprise clients in India. No notable security incidents documented.

Harafunnel.description: Harafunnel is a marketing automation tool that integrates with Facebook Messenger Bot to streamline customer engagement.
Harafunnel.history: Harafunnel is a Japanese marketing automation platform that integrates with Facebook Messenger to enable chatbot-based customer engagement and sales funnels. Founded around 2017, it primarily serves the Japanese market for e-commerce and digital marketing automation via Messenger bots.
Harafunnel.summary_abuse: Harafunnel is a Facebook Messenger bot marketing platform. Messenger bot marketing tools can be used for spam campaigns and unsolicited messaging. No specific documented abuse incidents, but Messenger-based marketing automation tools are periodically misused for bulk promotional messaging. Small Japanese vendor with no documented security program.

Haravan.description: Haravan is a multi-channel ecommerce services provider from Vietnam.
Haravan.history: Haravan was founded in March 2014 by Lamho Huynh in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. It started as a website builder for Vietnamese e-commerce businesses and expanded into a full omnichannel commerce platform serving SMBs in Vietnam, Thailand, and the Philippines. By 2026 it serves over 50,000 clients though year-over-year store counts declined around 15% in 2025.
Haravan.summary_abuse: Haravan is a regional e-commerce platform serving Vietnamese and Southeast Asian SMBs. Like any e-commerce platform, it could host counterfeit or fraudulent storefronts, but its focus on local SMB markets and known merchants limits the anonymous signup risk common on global platforms. No major abuse incidents have been publicly documented.

Harbor.description: Harbor is an open-source registry that secures artifacts with policies and role-based access control, ensures images are scanned and free from vulnerabilities, and signs images as trusted.
Harbor.history: Harbor is an open-source container image registry developed by VMware, first released in 2016. It was donated to the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) in 2018 and graduated as a CNCF project in 2020. It provides policy-based access control, vulnerability scanning, image signing, and replication for container images, and is widely used in Kubernetes environments.
Harbor.summary_abuse: Harbor is a self-hosted container registry with no consumer-facing surface. No documented abuse patterns. CVEs in Harbor have been reported (e.g., SSRF vulnerabilities in older versions) but are promptly patched. CNCF-graduated project with an active security disclosure process.

HarborByte.description: HarborByte is a small business website platform that provides templated sites with integrated ecommerce, marketing, and hosting solutions.
HarborByte.history: HarborByte is a small US-based website platform offering templated sites with integrated e-commerce, marketing, and hosting for small businesses. Very limited public presence and minimal market traction. Likely a micro-ISV or bootstrapped product.
HarborByte.summary_abuse: HarborByte is an obscure small business website builder with no documented abuse patterns. Very low adoption. No formal security program. Standard website hosting concerns apply.

Harness.description: Harness is a fundraising solution designed to streamline the process of raising funds for various causes and initiatives.
Harness.history: Harness (goharness.com) is a US-based SaaS fundraising platform designed for nonprofits, providing online donation tools, peer-to-peer fundraising, and donor management. Founded in the mid-2010s as a niche player in nonprofit technology. Not to be confused with Harness.io, the enterprise CI/CD platform.
Harness.summary_abuse: Harness is a nonprofit fundraising platform. Donation platforms can be targets for charity fraud and fake fundraiser scams. No specific documented abuse incidents. Handles payment processing for donations, so PCI compliance is a concern. Small vendor with limited public security documentation.

Hashnode.description: Hashnode is a free developer blogging platform that allows you to publish articles on your own domain and helps you stay connected with a global developer community.
Hashnode.history: Hashnode is a free developer blogging platform launched around 2020 that allows publishing articles on custom domains. It gained significant traction in the developer community as an alternative to Medium and Dev.to, positioning on developer ownership of content and custom domains. Raised $6.7M in Series A in 2022. Offers Headless Hashnode for headless CMS use cases.
Hashnode.summary_abuse: Hashnode is a developer blogging platform with a free tier supporting custom domains. As a content hosting platform with easy account creation, it has been used occasionally for SEO spam and low-quality AI-generated content. The free custom domain feature can be exploited for phishing landing pages. No systematic abuse documented, and Hashnode moderates content per their terms.

Hashtag Labs.description: Hashtag Labs is a full-service digital ad operations company.
Hashtag Labs.history: Hashtag Labs is a US-based digital ad operations company providing managed ad tech services including header bidding, ad stack management, and programmatic revenue optimization for publishers. Small boutique ad tech operation serving media publishers.
Hashtag Labs.summary_abuse: Hashtag Labs is a digital ad operations firm that manages programmatic advertising on publisher sites. Ad tech companies inherently involve third-party tracker injection and data collection across publisher sites. No specific documented abuse incidents, but ad ops companies operate in a space with systemic privacy concerns. Publishers should review data flows before integration.

HashThemes Total.description: HashThemes Total is the powerful and creative multipurpose WordPress theme.
HashThemes Total.history: HashThemes Total is a multipurpose WordPress theme developed by HashThemes, a Nepalese WordPress theme shop. The company has produced multiple free and premium WordPress themes since the mid-2010s, with Total being their flagship multipurpose offering.
HashThemes Total.summary_abuse: HashThemes Total is a WordPress theme from a Nepalese developer. No documented abuse patterns. WordPress themes can have security vulnerabilities, but no notable CVEs are documented for this theme. Standard WordPress theme security concerns apply.

Haskell.history: Haskell is a purely functional, statically-typed programming language with lazy evaluation. It was designed in 1987 by a committee of researchers aiming to unify functional programming research, with the first version standardized in 1990. Haskell 98 was a stability-focused standard. Haskell 2010 is the current standard. The language has been enormously influential on modern type system design (TypeScript, Rust, Swift) despite modest direct adoption.
Haskell.summary_abuse: Haskell is a programming language with no inherent abuse surface. No documented misuse patterns beyond those of any programming language. Foundational academic and research language.

Hasura.description: Hasura is an open-source engine that enables developers to effortlessly create real-time GraphQL APIs by auto-generating them from existing database schemas.
Hasura.history: Hasura was founded in 2018 and launched its open-source GraphQL engine that auto-generates a real-time GraphQL API from PostgreSQL schemas. It raised $25M Series B in 2020 and $100M Series C in 2022. Hasura DDN (Data Delivery Network) was launched in 2024 as a next-generation data access layer. The company is headquartered in San Francisco with origins in Bangalore, India.
Hasura.summary_abuse: Hasura is a backend infrastructure tool for building GraphQL APIs. No documented abuse patterns. As an API layer sitting in front of databases, proper authorization configuration is critical (misconfigured permissions could expose data), but this is a developer responsibility rather than a platform abuse concern. Strong security posture with SOC 2 Type 2 and bug bounty program.

Hatena Blog.description: Hatena Blog is one of the traditional blog platforms in Japan.
Hatena Blog.history: Hatena Blog is a major Japanese blogging platform operated by Hatena Co., Ltd., a Japanese internet company founded in 2001 known for its social bookmarking service (Hatena Bookmark). Hatena Blog launched in 2011 as a successor to Hatena Diary (launched 2003), and remains one of the most popular blogging platforms in Japan. Hatena Co. is listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.
Hatena Blog.summary_abuse: Hatena Blog is a major Japanese blogging platform. As a free hosting service, it has occasionally been used for spam SEO content and phishing pages targeting Japanese internet users. Hatena actively moderates content per Japanese legal requirements and their terms of service. Primary user base is Japanese-language content creators.

HawkSearch.description: HawkSearch is a responsive ecommerce site search solution designed to enhance product discovery and improve search functionality.
HawkSearch.history: HawkSearch is an AI-powered site search and product discovery platform specializing in B2B e-commerce. It is based in Des Plaines, Illinois with a small team. The company has completed over 800 implementations and serves manufacturers, distributors, and retailers needing advanced catalog search. Annual revenue reached $3.8M as of August 2025.
HawkSearch.summary_abuse: HawkSearch is a B2B enterprise search platform with no notable abuse profile. It provides search functionality to merchants and does not offer consumer-facing anonymous sign-up or user-generated content hosting. Abuse risk is negligible.

hCaptcha.description: hCaptcha is an anti-bot solution that protects user privacy and rewards websites.
hCaptcha.history: hCaptcha was launched in 2018 by Intuition Machines as a privacy-focused alternative to Google's reCAPTCHA. Cloudflare adopted hCaptcha in 2020 as its default CAPTCHA provider, providing significant growth. It distinguishes itself by paying website operators for solved CAPTCHAs through its data labeling business model.
hCaptcha.summary_abuse: hCaptcha is an anti-bot CAPTCHA service with no notable direct abuse signal. It is designed to prevent automated abuse rather than facilitate it. Some CAPTCHA-solving services target hCaptcha, but this is a generic risk for all CAPTCHA providers.

HCL Commerce.description: HCL Commerce is a software platform framework for ecommerce, including marketing, sales, customer and order processing functionality.
dom: link[href*='/wcsstore/'], link[href*='webapp/wcs/'], a[href*='/wcsstore/'], a[href*='webapp/wcs/'], script[src*='/wcsstore/'], script[src*='webapp/wcs/']
HCL Commerce.history: HCL Commerce (originally IBM WebSphere Commerce) was created by IBM in 1996. IBM sold the WebSphere Commerce product line to HCL Technologies in 2019 as part of a $1.8 billion deal covering several IBM software products. Under HCL, it was rebranded as HCL Commerce and continues to serve large enterprise retailers globally.
HCL Commerce.summary_abuse: HCL Commerce is enterprise e-commerce software with no notable abuse history. It is deployed by large retailers and brands on their own infrastructure with strict security controls.

HCL Digital Experience.description: HCL Digital Experience software empowers you to create, manage and deliver engaging omni-channel digital experiences to virtually all audiences.
HCL Digital Experience.history: HCL Digital Experience (originally IBM WebSphere Portal and IBM Web Content Manager) was developed by IBM from the late 1990s. IBM sold it to HCL Technologies in 2019 as part of the same $1.8 billion deal that included HCL Commerce. It continues as a leading enterprise portal and digital experience platform.
HCL Digital Experience.summary_abuse: HCL Digital Experience is enterprise portal software used by large organizations. No abuse history. It runs on customer-controlled infrastructure.

HCL Domino.description: HCL Domino, formerly called IBM Domino (1995) and Lotus Domino (1989), is an enterprise server application development platform.
HCL Domino.history: IBM Lotus Notes/Domino was originally created by Iris Associates in 1989 and acquired by Lotus Development Corporation, which IBM then acquired in 1995. It became a leading enterprise collaboration, email, and application development platform. IBM sold the Domino product line to HCL Technologies in 2019 for $1.8 billion. Under HCL, Domino has continued with regular major version releases, reversing a period of neglect under IBM.
HCL Domino.summary_abuse: HCL Domino is enterprise collaboration software deployed on-premises by large organizations. Historical vulnerabilities have been patched. Domino servers running outdated versions are occasionally found exposed on the internet, but HCL actively maintains the product with regular security updates.

Header Bidding Ai.description: Header Bidding Ai is a provider of an automated and managed header bidding solution. Header bidding cutting-edge technique where publishers offer their ad inventory to many ad exchanges.
Header Bidding Ai.history: Header Bidding Ai is an automated managed header bidding platform connecting publishers to ad buyers via a single script; limited public founding information.
Header Bidding Ai.summary_abuse: Header Bidding Ai is an ad monetization service that embeds ad auction scripts on publishers pages. As with most ad-tech, it ships trackers and third-party calls by default. Small ad-tech vendors can be vectors for malvertising, though no specific incidents are documented for this vendor.

HeadJS.description: HeadJS is a JavaScript library that optimizes web performance by managing the loading and execution of scripts and stylesheets to ensure faster page loads and efficient resource handling.
HeadJS.history: HeadJS is a JavaScript script-loading library created around 2010 by Tero Piirainen. It was designed to load scripts in parallel while executing in order and detect browser features. The project went dormant around 2014 as native HTML5 features and async/defer attributes replaced its functionality.
HeadJS.summary_abuse: HeadJS is an abandoned client-side script loader. No abuse surface beyond inclusion as a legacy dependency. No known security incidents.

Headless UI.description: Headless UI is an unstyled component library for either React.js or Vue.js from the same people that created Tailwind CSS.
Headless UI.history: Headless UI is an open-source unstyled, accessible UI component library for React and Vue, developed by Tailwind Labs (creators of Tailwind CSS). Released in 2020 to pair with Tailwind CSS for building accessible components without imposing visual styles. Widely adopted in the Tailwind CSS ecosystem.
Headless UI.summary_abuse: Headless UI is a frontend component library with no abuse surface. Pure client-side UI components. No documented misuse patterns.

Headroom.js.description: Headroom.js is a JS widget that allows you to react to the user's scroll. The header slides out of view when scrolling down and slides back in when scrolling up.
Headroom.js.history: Headroom.js is a lightweight JavaScript library created by Nick Williams around 2013 that hides/shows a page header based on scroll direction, improving mobile UX. Maintained as an open-source MIT project with modest but steady adoption for several years.
Headroom.js.summary_abuse: Headroom.js is a small client-side scroll-behavior library with no abuse surface. Foundational UI utility with no reported security issues.

Headway.description: Headway is a product widget that keeps customers informed about updates and changes.
Headway.history: Headway is a product changelog and announcement tool founded in 2015 by Oskar Krawczyk and Michal Laskowski. It is an unfunded, bootstrapped independent product that allows SaaS companies to publish embeddable changelogs. The Pro plan costs $29 per month and the free tier is permanently available.
Headway.summary_abuse: Headway is a changelog notification widget for SaaS products. It has no meaningful abuse profile, no consumer-facing anonymous sign-up, and no user content hosting that would attract abusers.

Heap.history: Heap was founded in 2013 as a product analytics platform that auto-captures all user interactions without manual event instrumentation. It raised $216M total across multiple rounds including a $110M Series D at a $960M valuation. In September 2023 Contentsquare announced it would acquire Heap, completing the deal in December 2023, calling it their largest acquisition to date.
Heap.summary_abuse: Heap is a product analytics platform that records user behaviour on customer websites. As an analytics tracker it collects user interaction data, which is an inherent privacy consideration. Heap is SOC2 and ISO 27001 certified and GDPR compliant. No significant abuse incidents have been publicly documented, though like any analytics platform its scripts could theoretically be used for fingerprinting.

Hearth.description: Hearth is a financial services platform that provides financing options for home improvement projects.
Hearth.history: Hearth is a US fintech startup founded around 2017 that provides financing options embedded in home improvement contractor websites. It allows contractors to offer customers instant financing for renovation projects. The platform connects homeowners with lenders via a white-label widget.
Hearth.summary_abuse: Hearth is a home improvement financing platform. Financing platforms embedded in contractor websites can be targets for fake contractor fraud. Handles consumer financial data and credit applications. As a fintech startup, regulatory compliance (consumer lending laws) is a concern. No notable security incidents documented.

Heartland Payment Systems.description: Heartland Payment Systems is a US-based payment processing and technology provider.
Heartland Payment Systems.history: Heartland Payment Systems was founded in 1997 as a US payment processing company. In 2008-2009 it suffered one of the largest payment card data breaches in history, exposing over 100 million card numbers via SQL injection on its processing systems. The company was acquired by Global Payments Inc. in 2016 for approximately $4.3 billion. It now operates as a subsidiary serving restaurants, hotels, education, and small businesses.
Heartland Payment Systems.summary_abuse: Heartland suffered a massive payment card breach in 2008 (100M+ cards compromised via SQL injection), one of the largest data breaches in US history at the time. The attacker, Albert Gonzalez, was convicted. Post-breach, Heartland invested heavily in security and introduced end-to-end encryption. Now owned by Global Payments, it maintains PCI-DSS certification and SOC 2. Ongoing vigilance required as payment processors remain targets for threat actors.

Heeet.description: Heeet is a lead tracking and marketing analytics tool.
Heeet.history: Heeet is a French lead tracking and marketing attribution analytics SaaS founded around 2017. It connects Salesforce CRM data with marketing spend to attribute revenue to marketing campaigns, particularly for B2B companies. Targets Salesforce-centric marketing teams.
Heeet.summary_abuse: Heeet is a B2B marketing analytics platform that deploys tracking scripts on customer websites. As a tracking tool, it collects behavioral data tied to marketing attribution. French company with likely GDPR compliance. No documented abuse patterns. Standard analytics tool concerns apply.

Heights Platform.description: Heights Platform is an all-in-one platform for creating and selling online courses.
Heights Platform.history: Heights Platform is a US-based SaaS for creators to build and sell online courses and coaching programs. Founded around 2018 as a bootstrapped alternative to Teachable and Thinkific, it positions on simplicity and creator-friendly pricing without transaction fees.
Heights Platform.summary_abuse: Heights Platform is an online course creation SaaS. No documented abuse patterns. Standard concerns around course content quality and refund policies apply, but no platform-level abuse issues documented. Small bootstrapped vendor.

Helhost.description: Helhost is a web hosting provider and internet domain registrar from Democratic Republic of Congo.
Helhost.history: Helhost is a web hosting provider and domain registrar based in the Democratic Republic of Congo, serving primarily the Central African market. Very small operation with minimal public presence outside the DRC region.
Helhost.summary_abuse: Helhost is a small web hosting provider from the Democratic Republic of Congo. Small regional hosting providers with minimal abuse monitoring infrastructure can be used to host malicious content. No documented specific incidents, but the combination of low visibility, limited resources, and no formal security program warrants caution. Abuse reporting responsiveness is unknown.

HeliumWeb.description: HeliumWeb is a server-side (backend) web framework written in PHP & JavaScript
HeliumWeb.history: HeliumWeb is an obscure PHP and JavaScript web framework with no significant public adoption or documentation. Very limited presence; likely a hobby or student project.
HeliumWeb.summary_abuse: HeliumWeb is an unknown/obscure web framework with negligible adoption. No consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse patterns.

Helix Ultimate.description: Helix Ultimate a free template framework for Joomla.
Helix Ultimate.history: Helix Ultimate is a free, open-source Joomla template framework developed by JoomShaper, a Bangladeshi Joomla template company. Released as an evolution of earlier Helix frameworks, it provides a base for building Joomla templates with a drag-and-drop layout editor. Widely used in the Joomla community.
Helix Ultimate.summary_abuse: Helix Ultimate is a Joomla template framework. No documented abuse patterns. Standard Joomla extension security concerns apply — users should keep it updated. No notable CVEs documented for this framework specifically.

Helixo UFE.description: Helixo UFE is a lightweight Shopify upsell sales funnel app.
Helixo UFE.history: Helixo UFE (Upsell Funnel Engine) is a Shopify app that adds upsell and cross-sell popups, one-click upsells, and sales funnel features to Shopify stores. Developed by a small Indian team and available on the Shopify App Store since around 2019.
Helixo UFE.summary_abuse: Helixo UFE is a Shopify upsell app. Upsell apps are designed to increase average order value through post-purchase offers, which can be used to create dark patterns (aggressive upsell popups, confusing decline buttons). No documented platform-level abuse. Users should review consent flows implemented via the app.

HelixPay.description: HelixPay is an event ticketing platform providing online payment integration, digital tickets, and customizable event websites for large-scale events.
HelixPay.history: HelixPay is a Philippine fintech startup providing event ticketing, online payment integration, and event management tools for large-scale events in the Philippines. Founded around 2018, it focuses on the Philippine market with local payment method support.
HelixPay.summary_abuse: HelixPay is a Philippine event ticketing and payment platform. Ticketing platforms can be targeted for scalping bots and fake event scams. No specific documented abuse incidents. Small Philippine startup with no formal security program documented.

Hello Bar.description: Hello Bar is a customizable notification bar that draws visitors to an important call to action on the website.
Hello Bar.history: Hello Bar was created by Neil Patel and co-founders around 2012 as a simple notification bar tool for websites. The company went through multiple acquisitions and ownership changes. It offers customizable top/bottom bars, popups, and sliders to drive email signups and conversions. Neil Patel later sold it; it is now independently operated.
Hello Bar.summary_abuse: Hello Bar is a website conversion tool delivering notification bars and popups. As a third-party script embedded on many sites, it collects visitor data and can display any content the site owner configures, including potentially deceptive UX. The free tier lowers the barrier to deploy it widely. No documented platform-level abuse by the vendor.

Hello Elementor.description: Hello Elementor is a WordPress theme built for the Elementor website builder platform. It uses minimal styling and scripts for maximum speed and design freedom.
Hello Elementor.history: Hello Elementor (also called Hello Theme) is the official free WordPress starter theme created by Elementor Ltd. (Israel) and released around 2019. It is designed as the canonical companion theme for the Elementor page builder plugin, providing a bare-bones starter with minimal code to pair with Elementor's drag-and-drop editor. It is one of the most installed WordPress themes.
Hello Elementor.summary_abuse: Hello Elementor is a free WordPress starter theme from Elementor. No documented abuse patterns. Widely deployed on WordPress sites across many categories. Elementor themes/plugins are popular targets for exploitation due to their widespread use, but Hello Elementor itself (being minimal code) has few CVEs. Users should keep it updated.

Hello Retail.description: Hello Retail is an AI-driven platform that delivers personalized product recommendations, intelligent search, and email solutions for ecommerce.
Hello Retail.history: Hello Retail is a Danish ecommerce personalization and AI product recommendations platform founded in 2012. It provides personalized search, product recommendations, and email personalization for online retailers. The company serves primarily Nordic and European e-commerce merchants.
Hello Retail.summary_abuse: Hello Retail is an e-commerce personalization platform that deploys tracking scripts on merchant sites. As a behavioral tracking and profiling tool, it collects detailed shopping behavior data. Danish company with strong GDPR compliance obligations. No documented abuse patterns. Standard third-party analytics script privacy concerns apply.

Help Scout.description: Help Scout is a customer service platform including email, a knowledge base tool and live chat.
Help Scout.history: Help Scout was founded in 2011 by Nick Francis, Denny Swindle, and Jared McDaniel in Boston, Massachusetts. It raised approximately $52.9M across multiple venture rounds, the latest in 2017. The company has grown to 140 employees and remains independent, providing email-based customer support tools designed to feel personal rather than transactional. No acquisitions have been made by or of Help Scout.
Help Scout.summary_abuse: Help Scout is a customer support platform for businesses. It has SOC2 Type 2 certification, GDPR and HIPAA compliance. No notable abuse incidents have been publicly documented. As a B2B tool with business account sign-up, it has minimal exposure to anonymous abuser sign-up or consumer-facing fraud.

Help.com.description: Help.com is a service that provides domain name registration and website hosting with simplified setup and management.
Help.com.history: Help.com is a US-based web hosting and domain registration service. The premium domain has changed hands and uses over the years. Currently operates as a simplified hosting and website setup provider.
Help.com.summary_abuse: Help.com is a web hosting and domain registration provider. Small hosting providers with minimal abuse monitoring can be used to host malicious content. Very limited public information. No documented specific incidents.

HelpCrunch.description: HelpCrunch is a customer service software platform designed to support core business communication and customer support needs across organizations.
HelpCrunch.history: HelpCrunch is a Ukrainian-developed customer communication platform that launched its MVP in 2016. It combines live chat, email marketing, and help desk ticketing in one subscription. The company received a $490K funding round in March 2020 and reached approximately $1.8M in annual revenue by 2025. HelpCrunch serves over 10,000 customers globally and is considered a Ukrainian-built SaaS success story.
HelpCrunch.summary_abuse: HelpCrunch is a B2B customer support platform with no notable abuse profile. It provides live chat and ticketing tools to businesses through authenticated accounts. The chat widget embedded on customer websites could theoretically receive spam messages, but this is standard for the category and not a platform-specific risk.

HelpDocs.description: HelpDocs is an knowledge management system.
HelpDocs.history: HelpDocs is a UK-based SaaS knowledge base and documentation platform founded around 2017. It provides teams with a hosted knowledge base for customer-facing documentation and internal wikis. Bootstrapped indie SaaS competing with Zendesk Guide, Intercom Articles, and Document360.
HelpDocs.summary_abuse: HelpDocs is a knowledge base SaaS. No documented abuse patterns. As a documentation hosting service, there is minimal abuse surface. Small UK bootstrapped vendor with GDPR compliance. No notable security incidents.

HelpOnClick.description: HelpOnClick is a live chat and customer tracking platform designed to support real-time communication and monitor visitor interactions on websites.
HelpOnClick.history: HelpOnClick is a live chat and visitor tracking SaaS platform, founded around 2009 and likely Israeli in origin. It provides real-time chat widgets, visitor monitoring, and basic CRM features for websites. A relatively small and mature live chat platform serving small businesses.
HelpOnClick.summary_abuse: HelpOnClick is a live chat platform with visitor tracking capabilities. As a chat widget provider, it has access to site visitor data. No documented abuse patterns. Small vendor with no formal security program. Standard live chat data privacy considerations apply.

Helpwise.description: Helpwise is a customer service platform that helps you manage all customer communication from a single place.
Helpwise.history: Helpwise is an Indian SaaS startup founded around 2018 that provides a shared inbox platform for teams to manage customer communications from email, WhatsApp, SMS, and social channels in one place. Competing with Freshdesk, Help Scout, and Front. The company raised seed funding and targets SMBs.
Helpwise.summary_abuse: Helpwise is a shared inbox and customer service platform. No documented abuse patterns. As an email and messaging platform, it is subject to standard concerns around spam if misused. Indian startup with GDPR compliance claimed. No notable security incidents documented.

Here.description: HERE is a PaaS for creating custom maps, visualize location datasets, gather insights and buy and sell location assets.
Here.history: HERE was founded in 1985 as Navteq, a digital map data company. Nokia acquired it in 2008 for $8.1 billion. In 2015, a consortium of German automakers (BMW, Audi, Mercedes-Benz) acquired HERE from Nokia for approximately 2.8 billion euros. Bosch and Continental each acquired 5% stakes in 2018.
Here.summary_abuse: HERE Technologies provides mapping and location APIs used by developers and enterprises. The platform's geolocation and mapping APIs could be used in tracking or surveillance applications, but HERE operates under enterprise contracts with legitimate use cases. No documented large-scale abuse incidents are known.

Hermes.description: Hermes offers integrated solutions along the supply chain and partners with national and international trading companies.
Hermes.history: Hermes was founded in 1972 in Germany as a parcel and logistics service. It operates as a subsidiary of the Otto Group, one of Germany's largest retail and logistics conglomerates. The company focuses primarily on the German and European markets for parcel delivery and supply chain services.
Hermes.summary_abuse: Hermes is a German logistics and shipping carrier operating under the Otto Group. It provides parcel delivery integration for e-commerce platforms. No notable abuse signal is associated with this technology. It is used by legitimate online retailers for order fulfillment and shipping carrier integration.

Hero.description: Hero is a virtual shopping platform for ecommerce and retail stores.
Hero.history: Hero was founded as a virtual shopping platform connecting online shoppers with store associates. Klarna acquired Hero in July 2021 to enhance its social shopping capabilities. In 2024, Klarna sold Hero to Bambuser, a Swedish live video commerce company, where it continues to operate.
Hero.summary_abuse: Hero is a B2B virtual shopping platform used by retail brands. It operates under enterprise agreements and has no notable abuse signal. The platform connects shoppers with retail staff via video chat and is not a surface commonly targeted for fraud or misuse.

Heroic.description: Heroic is a platform that offers design templates and tools for creating websites.
Heroic.history: Founded in 2014 by Marisa Murgatroyd and Murray Gray, targeting coaches and consultants. In July 2021, the platform underwent new ownership and a service redesign.
Heroic.summary_abuse: Heroic is a niche website builder for coaches and consultants with limited consumer-facing surface. Its page-builder features could theoretically be used for fake business sites, but no documented abuse pattern is associated with the platform. Legitimate use overwhelmingly dominates.

Heroku.description: Heroku is a cloud platform as a service (PaaS) supporting several programming languages.
Heroku.history: Heroku was founded in 2007 by Orion Henry, James Lindenbaum, and Adam Wiggins as a Ruby PaaS. Salesforce acquired it in December 2010 for approximately $212 million. After years as a developer favorite, Heroku removed its free tier in 2022, causing significant community backlash and migration to alternatives like Render and Railway.
Heroku.summary_abuse: Heroku is a well-known PaaS platform used to host web applications. It has historically been abused to host phishing pages and malware C2 servers due to its free tier (which was removed in 2022) and ease of deployment. Since removing the free tier, abuse reports have declined significantly, though paid accounts can still be misused. Salesforce maintains an AUP and abuse reporting mechanisms.

HeroUI.description: HeroUI allows you to make beautiful, modern, and fast websites/applications regardless of your design experience, created with React.js and Stitches, based on React Aria and inspired by Vuesax.
HeroUI.history: HeroUI (formerly NextUI) is a React component library created by Junior Garcia, first released around 2021. It was originally named NextUI but rebranded to HeroUI. It provides accessible, customizable UI components built on top of React Aria and Tailwind CSS.
HeroUI.summary_abuse: HeroUI is a React component library with no abuse signal. It is a frontend development tool used to build user interfaces and has no consumer-facing hosted surface that could be exploited.

Hesk.description: Hesk is a helpdesk and customer support system.
Hesk.history: HESK is a free PHP-based help desk software created by Klemen Stirn, a Slovenian developer. The free self-hosted version has been available since approximately 2005. Paid license editions were added for commercial use. HESK has remained a solo-developer product, consistently popular for small businesses seeking an inexpensive self-hosted support ticket system.
Hesk.summary_abuse: HESK has minimal abuse signal. It is a self-hosted help desk application installed on the operator's own server. As backend support software with no public hosting surface, it is not a meaningful target for fraud or phishing. Vulnerable old versions have occasionally appeared in CVE databases but this is a maintenance issue rather than deliberate abuse.

Hestia.description: Hestia is a modern WordPress theme for professionals a multipurpose one-page design, widgetized footer, blog/news page, and a clean look.
Hestia.history: Hestia is a free WordPress theme developed by ThemeIsle, a Romanian WordPress theme and plugin company. It was released around 2016 as a multipurpose one-page WordPress theme. ThemeIsle continues to maintain and update it.
Hestia.summary_abuse: Hestia is a WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. As a theme distributed via WordPress.org and ThemeIsle, it represents a standard website template and is not associated with fraudulent activity.

HetrixTools.description: HetrixTools is an uptime and blacklist monitoring platform.
HetrixTools.history: HetrixTools launched in May 2015 as a bootstrapped uptime and blacklist monitoring service; no notable acquisitions or ownership changes since.
HetrixTools.summary_abuse: HetrixTools is a backend monitoring tool with no content-hosting or user-facing surface. It has no notable abuse signal; it monitors IPs and domains rather than hosting them, so fraudsters have no meaningful leverage.

Hetzner.description: Hetzner provides dedicated hosting, shared web hosting, virtual private servers, managed servers, domain names, SSL certificates, storage boxes, and cloud.
Hetzner.history: Hetzner Online was founded in 1997 in Germany by Martin Hetzner. It grew from a shared hosting provider to one of Europe's largest and most cost-effective cloud and dedicated hosting providers. It remains privately owned and has not undergone major acquisitions.
Hetzner.summary_abuse: Hetzner is a German hosting and cloud provider known for aggressive pricing. It has been repeatedly cited in threat intelligence reports for hosting malware, phishing infrastructure, and botnet command-and-control servers. Its low prices attract both legitimate users and threat actors. Hetzner maintains an abuse contact and AUP but has been criticized for slow takedown response relative to larger providers.

Hexia.description: Hexia is a public Application Programming Interface (API) provided by Hex Technologies for accessing its services and data.
Hexia.history: Hexia is a small API platform by Hex Technologies providing developer access to Hex analytics services; minimal public history available.
Hexia.summary_abuse: Hexia is a developer API with no consumer-facing content hosting surface. No documented abuse signal.

Hexo.description: Hexo is a blog framework powered by Node.js.
Hexo.history: Hexo is a fast, simple Node.js-powered blog framework and static site generator created by Tommy Chen and first released in 2012. Originally popular in the Chinese developer community, it gained wider international adoption. The project is maintained by a small team of open-source volunteers and hosted under the hexojs GitHub organization. It generates static files deployable to any static hosting provider.
Hexo.summary_abuse: Hexo is a static site generator that produces plain HTML/CSS/JS files run locally by the developer. It has no server component, no user accounts, no hosting service, and no abuse surface. It has no meaningful abuse profile.

Hextom Free Shipping Bar.description: Free Shipping Bar is a Shopify app built by Hextom. Free Shipping Bar help promote free shipping with progressive messages to motivate customers to buy more.
Hextom Free Shipping Bar.history: Hextom Free Shipping Bar was launched on September 16, 2015 by Hextom Inc., a Toronto-based Shopify app company founded by engineers. The app displays progressive free shipping messages to Shopify store customers and has grown to be installed on over 41,000 stores with a 4.9-star rating from nearly 3,000 reviews. Hextom serves over 270,000 merchants across its app portfolio.
Hextom Free Shipping Bar.summary_abuse: Hextom Free Shipping Bar is a Shopify conversion tool that displays promotional banners on merchant storefronts. It has no consumer-facing sign-up, no user-generated content, and no meaningful abuse surface. As a Shopify-distributed app it is subject to Shopify's developer policies.

Hextom Ultimate Sales Boost.description: Ultimate Sales Boost by Hextom is an app designed to help you drive more sales by creating a sense of urgency, scarcity and trust.
Hextom Ultimate Sales Boost.history: Hextom Ultimate Sales Boost (rebranded to Hextom Upsell Sales Boost) is a Shopify app developed by Hextom Inc., a Toronto-based engineer-founded company. It offers over 30 conversion tools including countdown timers, trust badges, upsell prompts, and BOGO offers. The app has been available since around 2016 and is one of Hextom's flagship products alongside the Free Shipping Bar.
Hextom Ultimate Sales Boost.summary_abuse: Hextom Ultimate Sales Boost is a Shopify conversion optimization tool. It injects promotional widgets like countdown timers and trust badges on merchant storefronts, which are common conversion tactics. Dark pattern risk is moderate given countdown timers can be used manipulatively, though this is a merchant configuration choice. No significant abuse incidents have been documented for the app itself.

Heyflow.description: Heyflow is an all-in-one platform that enables users to create interactive lead funnels, multi-step forms, and customized landing pages that drive conversions and engage website visitors, all without the need for coding.
Heyflow.history: Heyflow was founded in 2020 in Hamburg, Germany as a no-code platform for creating interactive lead-generation funnels and click flows. The company raised €14.7 million in venture funding and has grown to approximately 86 employees with $9.5 million in annual revenue by 2024. Heyflow operates as an independent VC-backed startup and has not undergone acquisitions or rebranding.
Heyflow.summary_abuse: Heyflow is a no-code flow builder used for lead generation landing pages and forms. Like other no-code page builders, it could theoretically be used to create phishing landing pages, but its subscription pricing (no free tier) and GDPR-focused German regulatory environment limit anonymous abuse. No specific incidents of large-scale phishing or fraud have been publicly reported for Heyflow.

HeyLight.description: HeyLight is a BNPL system allowing instalment payments for online purchases.
HeyLight.history: HeyLight is a multi-country Buy Now Pay Later platform launched by Compass Banca, Italy's leading consumer credit company (a subsidiary of Mediobanca). It was built on the foundation of HeidiPay, a Swiss BNPL fintech acquired by Compass in 2023, and PagoLight, Compass's Italian BNPL operation. The platform operates in Italy with over 1,000 digital shops and 26,000 points of sale, and in Switzerland with over 850 retailers.
HeyLight.summary_abuse: HeyLight is a regulated BNPL and consumer credit platform operating under Mediobanca's Compass subsidiary. As a licensed payment institution with bank-level KYC and credit underwriting, abuse risk is low. The service offers installment financing to verified businesses and consumers, making it unsuitable as a fraud infrastructure tool. No notable public abuse incidents have been documented.

HeyPongo.description: HeyPongo is a platform that helps merchants convert visitors into loyal customers and boost revenue through customer engagement tools.
HeyPongo.history: HeyPongo is a French customer engagement and loyalty platform for retailers and e-merchants, serving over 2,000 merchants; no notable acquisitions or ownership changes found.
HeyPongo.summary_abuse: HeyPongo is a loyalty and customer engagement tool for retail merchants with no significant abuse signal. Its features are not typically exploited by fraudsters.

HeySummit.description: HeySummit is a platform facilitating the organization of virtual summits.
HeySummit.history: Founded in 2019 by Benjamin Dell after his frustrations with digital agency tools for running virtual events. The platform targets creators and educators hosting online summits and webinars.
HeySummit.summary_abuse: HeySummit is an online event hosting platform primarily used by educators and creators. It could theoretically host fake events to collect payments, but no documented abuse pattern has been reported. Legitimate use dominates.

HHVM.history: HHVM (HipHop Virtual Machine) was created by Facebook starting in 2010 and open-sourced in 2013 as a JIT-compiling PHP runtime aiming to replace Zend for performance. Facebook introduced the Hack language alongside it in 2014. In 2018, HHVM announced it would drop support for PHP 7+ compatibility to focus exclusively on Hack, causing most PHP shops (including Wikipedia) to migrate away. Meta continues developing HHVM internally for its Hack-language codebase.
HHVM.summary_abuse: HHVM is a backend runtime with no consumer-facing surface. No documented abuse patterns. Its primary use context is server-side PHP/Hack execution, providing no abuse vectors beyond those of any JIT runtime.

Hi Platform.description: Hi Platform provider of an online customer relationship platform.
Hi Platform.history: Hi Platform was formed in 2017 through the merger of Direct Talk (a Brazilian digital customer-service leader) and Seekr. The combined company is Brazil's largest customer-relationship and engagement platform, having raised R$35 million in funding.
Hi Platform.summary_abuse: Hi Platform is an omnichannel customer service platform focused on the Brazilian market. It has no notable abuse signal; its use case is enterprise customer communication rather than consumer-facing content hosting.

Hiawatha.description: Hiawatha is a secure, lightweight web server developed by Hugo Leisink, designed for ease of use and advanced security features such as protection against SQL injections, XSS, and CSRF attacks.
Hiawatha.history: Hiawatha is an open-source web server created by Dutch developer Hugo Leisink, initially released around 2002. It was designed as a lightweight, security-focused alternative to Apache and Nginx. The project has seen minimal activity in recent years and appears largely dormant.
Hiawatha.summary_abuse: Hiawatha is a self-hosted open-source web server with no notable abuse signal. It has no consumer-facing hosted service and is used by technically sophisticated operators who deploy it on their own infrastructure.

HiBob.description: HiBob is a modern HR platform designed to enhance productivity, employee engagement, and retention for businesses.
HiBob.history: HiBob was founded in 2015 in Tel Aviv, Israel by Ronni Zehavi and Israel David. It raised significant venture capital and grew rapidly as a modern HRIS competitor to legacy HR software. It has expanded to offices in London, New York, and other cities.
HiBob.summary_abuse: HiBob is an enterprise HR platform handling sensitive employee data including compensation, performance, and personal information. It is a B2B platform with no public-facing consumer surface. No notable abuse signal is associated with this technology.

Hibu.description: Hibu is an AI-enabled platform that helps generate, manage, respond to, and convert leads while supporting ongoing customer engagement through a single provider.
Hibu.history: Hibu traces its roots to Merchant Advertising Services (1930), later Yellow Book USA. The company rebranded as Hibu in May 2012 to reflect its pivot from print yellow-pages directories to digital marketing services for local US small businesses.
Hibu.summary_abuse: Hibu is a digital marketing agency for small businesses offering websites, SEO, and advertising. No notable abuse signal; it operates as a managed service with onboarded business clients rather than exposing consumer-facing self-serve hosting.

Highcharts.description: Highcharts is a charting library written in pure JavaScript, for adding interactive charts to a website or web application. Highcharts meets accessibility standards and works with Python, Angular, React, iOS, Android, and more.
Highcharts.history: Highcharts was created by Torstein Honsi in 2009 as a personal project in Norway, then developed into the Highsoft company. It grew to be used by 80 of the world's 100 largest companies, expanding into Highcharts Stock, Maps, Gantt, and Dashboards products. Free for non-commercial use, commercial licenses required for business use.
Highcharts.summary_abuse: Highcharts is a client-side JavaScript charting library with no consumer-facing attack surface. It renders charts in browsers and has no account system, hosting capabilities, or data collection features that would make it useful for abuse. No documented abuse history.

Higher Logic.description: Higher Logic is a digital marketing solution provider, formerly known as Informz.
Higher Logic.history: Founded in 2007 by Rob Wenger and Andy Steggles in Arlington, Virginia, to build community and engagement software for associations. Grew through five acquisitions between 2017 and 2020 (Socious, Kavi, Real Magnet, Informz, Customer Imperative), then acquired Vanilla in 2021 to expand into the corporate B2C market.
Higher Logic.summary_abuse: Higher Logic is a B2B community and engagement platform serving associations and enterprises. No notable abuse signal; it operates as a managed community solution for vetted organizational clients.

Higher Logic Vanilla.description: Higher Logic Vanilla is a cloud-based customer community platform offering features like discussion forums, Q&A, ideation, gamification, and advanced analytics, enabling businesses to enhance customer engagement and support.
Higher Logic Vanilla.history: Vanilla was founded in 2009 in Montreal, Canada as a cloud-based community platform. Higher Logic acquired Vanilla in May 2021, rebranding it as Higher Logic Vanilla to serve the corporate B2C community market.
Higher Logic Vanilla.summary_abuse: Higher Logic Vanilla is a managed community platform for enterprise B2C brands. No notable abuse signal; community moderation is built into the product and customers are vetted enterprise organizations.

HigherMe HR.description: HigherMe HR is a hiring software platform designed for hourly workforces, supporting job postings, applicant screening, interview scheduling, and onboarding workflows.
HigherMe HR.history: HigherMe was founded in 2014 by Jef Chedeville and Rob Hunter in Toronto, Canada, initially as a Y Combinator-backed startup targeting restaurant and retail hiring. The platform focuses on franchise restaurant chains.
HigherMe HR.summary_abuse: HigherMe is an HR recruiting platform for restaurant and retail employers with no notable abuse signal. The platform is B2B-only with no consumer-facing content hosting.

HighLevel.description: HighLevel is an all-in-one marketing and automation platform designed for marketing agencies and small businesses to manage CRM, marketing campaigns, sales funnels, appointment scheduling, and more.
HighLevel.history: HighLevel (GoHighLevel) was founded in 2018 by Shaun Clark, Varun Vairavan, and Robin Alex as an all-in-one CRM and marketing automation platform designed primarily for digital marketing agencies. The platform allows agencies to white-label and resell the entire suite to their clients. It grew rapidly in the agency market, reaching significant revenue without traditional VC funding initially, later raising investment. The platform is known as GHL in its community.
HighLevel.summary_abuse: HighLevel is an all-in-one marketing platform with SMS, email, and phone capabilities that creates meaningful spam risk. The platform has been associated with unsolicited SMS and email marketing campaigns sent through agency accounts. Because agencies can white-label and resell the service, enforcement of spam policies can be inconsistent. There are reports of bulk unsolicited text campaigns originating from GHL-powered systems. The platform includes mass email and SMS blasting features that require responsible use.

Highlight.io.description: Highlight.io is a monitoring software for developers, offering visibility into applications with features like session replay, error monitoring, and logging to identify frontend and backend issues.
Highlight.io.history: Highlight.io is an open-source monitoring platform founded around 2021. It offers session replay, error tracking, and logging as both a cloud SaaS and a self-hostable solution, positioning itself as an open-source alternative to tools like LogRocket and Datadog.
Highlight.io.summary_abuse: Highlight.io is a developer monitoring tool for application observability. It collects session data and application logs for developers. No notable abuse signal is associated with this technology.

Highlight.js.description: Highlight.js is a JavaScript library for syntax highlighting, supporting 192 languages and 496 themes.
Highlight.js.history: Highlight.js was created by Ivan Sagalaev and first released in 2006. It has become one of the most widely used syntax highlighting libraries, supporting hundreds of programming languages and themes. The project is maintained by the community on GitHub.
Highlight.js.summary_abuse: Highlight.js is a JavaScript syntax highlighting library with no abuse signal. It is a frontend developer tool with no consumer-facing hosted surface and no features relevant to fraudulent activity.

Highstock.history: Highstock is a financial charting library from Highcharts, a Norwegian company. Highcharts was founded by Torstein Honsi in 2009. Highstock extends Highcharts specifically for stock and general timeline charts with features like navigator, scrollbar, and date range selection.
Highstock.summary_abuse: Highstock is a JavaScript charting library for financial data visualization. It has no consumer-facing hosted surface and no abuse signal. It is embedded by developers in web applications to render stock and time-series charts.

HighStore.description: HighStore is an ecommerce platform from Iran.
HighStore.history: HighStore is an Iranian ecommerce platform hosted under digitalserver.ir; minimal public history is available.
HighStore.summary_abuse: HighStore is a niche ecommerce platform based in Iran. Due to limited public information and Iran-based hosting, trust signals are weak, but no specific documented abuse campaigns have been attributed to the platform.

Hikari CS.description: Hicari CS is a solution that helps webshops increase sales and customer loyalty with excellent customer service.
Hikari CS.history: Hikari CS is a Netherlands-based customer service outsourcing platform for online stores, offering multilingual support across chat and messaging channels on a pay-per-conversation model; no notable ownership changes found.
Hikari CS.summary_abuse: Hikari CS is a customer service platform for webshops with no notable abuse signal. The service handles customer queries rather than hosting consumer content.

HikaShop.description: HikaShop is a Joomla extension for managing shop content, handling price management, and customizing store views with an intuitive interface.
HikaShop.history: HikaShop is a Joomla and WordPress ecommerce extension by Hikari Software, a French team that has maintained the product for over 15 years. A free Starter edition and paid Essential/Business editions have been available since around 2010.
HikaShop.summary_abuse: HikaShop is a self-hosted ecommerce plugin for Joomla. As with any ecommerce extension, compromised Joomla installations running HikaShop could be used for fake storefronts, but no specific abuse campaigns target the extension itself.

HikeOrders.description: HikeOrders is a web accessibility overlay that claims to make your site disability friendly.
HikeOrders.history: HikeOrders is a web accessibility overlay product that provides an automated toolbar to help websites claim ADA/WCAG compliance. It is distributed under the WITHES framework branding and offers Shopify apps. Accessibility overlays as a category have faced significant industry criticism from accessibility experts who argue they do not achieve genuine compliance and may actually harm users with disabilities.
HikeOrders.summary_abuse: HikeOrders is a web accessibility overlay that enables websites to claim ADA/WCAG compliance through an automated JavaScript widget. The broader accessibility overlay industry has been widely criticized by the disability community and security researchers for providing false compliance assurances, potentially exposing site operators to legal liability. No specific fraud or phishing abuse incidents documented for HikeOrders specifically.

Hinza Advanced CMS.history: Hinza Advanced CMS is a lesser-known content management system from Hinza Co. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Hinza Advanced CMS.summary_abuse: Hinza Advanced CMS has no documented abuse signal; it is an obscure CMS with very limited user base.

HiPay.description: HiPay is a payment gateway provider and payment orchestration platform.
HiPay.history: HiPay originated from Hi-Media Group, a French digital advertising company. It became independent in 2015 when it separated from Hi-Media and was listed on Euronext Paris after approval by French regulator AMF. The company offers payment services across Europe with offices in France, Italy, Belgium and Portugal. In 2018 it launched an omnichannel payment solution covering online and in-store transactions.
HiPay.summary_abuse: HiPay is a regulated European payment service provider licensed by the French financial authority. As a PCI-DSS compliant payment processor with strong KYC requirements, it is not a notable abuse vector. Payments businesses generally face standard fraud risks such as card testing and chargeback fraud, which HiPay mitigates through its machine learning risk management engine.

Hireology.description: Hireology is a staffing and hiring platform for the franchise and retail-automotive industries.
Hireology.history: Hireology was founded in 2010 in Chicago, Illinois as an HR and hiring platform focused on franchise and multi-location businesses such as automotive dealerships and hospitality groups.
Hireology.summary_abuse: Hireology is a B2B HR platform with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

Hirschmann HiOS.description: Hirschmann HiOS is an operating system for industrial network equipment.
Hirschmann HiOS.history: HiOS (Hirschmann Operating System) is the firmware for Hirschmann's industrial Ethernet switches, developed by Hirschmann Automation and Control GmbH, which is part of Belden Inc. HiOS succeeded the Classic software line and added TSN and OPC UA capabilities for Industry 4.0 deployments.
Hirschmann HiOS.summary_abuse: Hirschmann HiOS is industrial network switch firmware deployed in factory and infrastructure automation environments. It has no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal.

Histats.description: Histats is a simple website visitor analysis and tracking tool.
Histats.history: Histats is a free web analytics and hit counter service that has been running since approximately 2006. It is one of the older free analytics services still operational, targeting small website owners and bloggers who want basic visitor tracking without complexity. The company appears to be small or solo-operated, based in Italy, and has maintained consistent service over many years without notable ownership changes.
Histats.summary_abuse: Histats embeds a tracking script on websites that collects visitor data including IP addresses, browser info, and geolocation. As a free analytics service with no strong KYC or account vetting, it can be embedded on any site including malicious ones, though it is not typically used as an active abuse tool. The service collects visitor data that is shared with the Histats platform, raising some privacy concerns for end users of sites using the tracker.

History.history: The history.js library was developed by the React Training team (notably Ryan Florence and Michael Jackson) to manage browser session history in JavaScript single-page applications. It became a foundational dependency for React Router and is widely used in React ecosystems.
History.summary_abuse: History.js is a JavaScript library for managing browser session history. It has no consumer-facing hosted surface and no abuse signal. It is a frontend utility used by developers building single-page applications.

Hit-Mall.description: Hit-Mall is a Japanese ecommerce solution designed to support online retail operations for businesses across various industries.
Hit-Mall.history: Hit-Mall is a Japanese ecommerce SaaS for online retail; minimal English-language public history is available.
Hit-Mall.summary_abuse: Hit-Mall is a Japanese ecommerce platform with no notable international abuse signal. Legitimate retail use dominates.

Hiver.description: Hiver is a tool that transforms Gmail into a collaboration and customer support system.
Hiver.history: Hiver was founded in 2011 in Bangalore, India, originally as GrexIt. It rebranded to Hiver and pivoted to a Gmail-based shared inbox and customer support tool. It has raised several rounds of venture funding and grown to serve thousands of teams globally.
Hiver.summary_abuse: Hiver is a Gmail-based team collaboration and customer support tool. It is used by businesses to manage shared inboxes and customer emails. No notable abuse signal is associated with this technology.

Hocalwire.description: Hocalwire is a simplified newsroom CMS platform focused on Digital Media Publishers.
Hocalwire.history: Hocalwire was founded in 2015 by Suresh K Jangir, Ashwani K Mishra, and Vinoth Poovalingam in Noida, India as a SaaS digital newsroom CMS for local news publishers.
Hocalwire.summary_abuse: Hocalwire is a digital newsroom CMS targeting news publishers with no notable abuse signal. The platform manages editorial workflows rather than hosting arbitrary user content.

HockeyStack.description: HockeyStack is a San Francisco-based analytics and attribution tool for B2B companies.
HockeyStack.history: Founded in 2021 by three Turkish co-founders who dropped out of US colleges. Participated in Y Combinator's 2023 batch, raised a $20M Series A from Bessemer Venture Partners in January 2025, growing revenue 4.5x in 2024.
HockeyStack.summary_abuse: HockeyStack is a B2B analytics and revenue attribution platform with no notable abuse signal. It processes business marketing and sales data on behalf of enterprise clients rather than hosting consumer content.

Hoefler&Co.description: Hoefler&Co is a digital type foundry (font design studio) in Woburn, Massachusetts (formerly New York City), founded by type designer Jonathan Hoefler. Hoefler&Co designs typefaces for clients and for retail on its website.
Hoefler&Co.history: Hoefler&Co (H&Co) is a prestigious digital type foundry founded by Jonathan Hoefler in New York in 1989, known for creating typefaces such as Hoefler Text, Gotham, and Tungsten used by major brands and publications. It relocated from New York to Woburn, Massachusetts.
Hoefler&Co.summary_abuse: Hoefler&Co is a premium font licensing service. Font piracy is the only relevant abuse vector, but that affects the vendor rather than enabling harm to end-users. No notable abuse signal of concern to end-users.

Hogan.js.description: Hogan.js is a JavaScript templating engine developed by Twitter, designed for both client-side and server-side applications, and it follows the Mustache template syntax for efficient and flexible template rendering.
Hogan.js.history: Developed by Twitter engineers Robert Sayre and Jacob Thornton and first released in 2011. Hogan.js is a compiler for the Mustache templating language, designed for fast client-side and server-side template rendering with separate scanning, parsing, and code generation phases. Twitter officially marked the project as unmaintained via its open-source status badge; the last release (v3.0.2) was in June 2014.
Hogan.js.summary_abuse: Hogan.js has no notable abuse signal. It is a JavaScript Mustache templating library with no consumer-facing surface, no content-hosting capabilities, no custom domain support, and no user-generated content features that fraudsters could exploit. As a backend/frontend rendering library with no service component, it has no documented history of abuse by phishing, scams, or malware operations.

Hoiio.description: Hoiio is a cloud communications platform that simplifies the setup of messaging, voice, and other communication services.
Hoiio.history: Hoiio was founded on 18 April 2007 in Singapore as a cloud communications and telephony API provider. It expanded regionally to Malaysia, Hong Kong, and Vietnam, and launched its developer API in 2012.
