Hoiio.summary_abuse: Hoiio is a cloud communications and voice API platform. Communications APIs can be abused for SMS spam, voice robocalls, or OTP bypass attacks, though no specific documented abuse campaigns target Hoiio directly. Legitimate business use dominates.

HoldMyTicket.description: HoldMyTicket is a platform offering event ticketing and box office solutions for organizers.
HoldMyTicket.history: HoldMyTicket was founded around 2009 in Albuquerque, New Mexico, as an event ticketing and box office solution targeting venues, festivals, and independent promoters. The company has operated for over 15 years as a zero-cost ticketing platform for organizers, charging service fees to ticket buyers instead of organizers. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
HoldMyTicket.summary_abuse: HoldMyTicket is an event ticketing platform. Ticketing platforms can be used to sell tickets to fake events as a fraud vector, though no specific large-scale incidents are documented for HoldMyTicket. The platform serves independent venues and festivals in a niche regional market, limiting its overall exposure to organized abuse.

Holduix CMS.description: Holduix CMS is a lightweight content management solution designed to offer developers and end users a customizable experience.
Holduix CMS.history: Holduix CMS is a lightweight content management system designed for developers, offering a customizable self-hosted or SaaS option. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Holduix CMS.summary_abuse: Holduix CMS has no documented abuse signal; it is a niche developer-oriented CMS.

HollerBox.description: HollerBox is a lead generation popup tool for WordPress.
HollerBox.history: HollerBox is an open-source WordPress popup and lead generation plugin originally created by Scott Bolinger and later maintained by Groundhogg Inc. It is fully self-hosted, GPL-licensed, free for unlimited use, and currently serves 6,000+ businesses.
HollerBox.summary_abuse: HollerBox has no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted WordPress popup plugin with GDPR-friendly features. Popup tools generally carry minor risk of misuse for dark patterns, but no documented incidents are specific to HollerBox.

Homebot.description: Homebot is a platform that converts client engagement into measurable financial transactions, enabling actionable insights and improved business outcomes.
Homebot.history: Homebot is a US fintech SaaS platform founded around 2015 that provides homeowners with automated home equity insights, helping mortgage lenders and real estate agents retain clients.
Homebot.summary_abuse: Homebot is a B2B fintech tool for mortgage and real estate professionals with no notable abuse signal. It does not expose consumer-facing content hosting.

Homefiniti.description: Homefiniti is a platform for home builders that offers tools for websites, virtual tours, and other marketing solutions.
Homefiniti.history: Homefiniti is the flagship product of ONeil Interactive, a Maryland-based web design and marketing agency specializing in the home building industry. Founded by Dennis O'Neil, a building industry veteran, the platform provides CMS, lead management, XML syndication, and interactive floor plans for home builders. The Homefiniti trademark was filed in December 2019. No notable funding events or ownership changes are documented.
Homefiniti.summary_abuse: Homefiniti is a niche marketing platform for home builders with no consumer-facing content hosting surface. It has no documented abuse signal.

Homerr.description: Homerr is a logistics company operating in the Netherlands and Belgium.
Homerr.history: Homerr was founded in December 2016 in Amsterdam by Juriaan Matthijssen, Mark-Jan Pieterse, and Theo Hoeksema as a last-mile parcel pick-up/drop-off network in the Netherlands. It was acquired by Vinted Go in October 2023 and rebranded as Vinted Go in Benelux markets in March 2024.
Homerr.summary_abuse: Homerr is a parcel logistics platform with no notable abuse signal. Physical package delivery services are not typically exploited for digital fraud.

Homerun.description: Homerun is a platform used to organize hiring processes and manage candidate applications.
Homerun.history: Homerun was founded in 2015 in Amsterdam by Willem van Roosmalen, Thomas Moes, and Bob Kreefft as a design-focused ATS for SMBs. Acquired by ISH Holding in 2025; the same year Homerun merged with Staff Squared to launch Homerun HR.
Homerun.summary_abuse: Homerun is a B2B applicant tracking system with no notable abuse signal. It processes hiring workflows for SMBs without a consumer-facing content surface.

Homestead.history: Homestead Technologies was founded in 1997 and launched a drag-and-drop website builder for small businesses in 1998. Intuit acquired it for approximately $170 million in 2007. In 2012, Intuit sold the website business to Endurance International Group, under which Homestead continues to operate.
Homestead.summary_abuse: Homestead is a legacy small-business website builder now owned by Endurance International Group. As a general-purpose page builder, it could theoretically host fake business sites, but it is primarily used by legitimate small businesses with no documented significant abuse campaigns.

HomHero.description: Homhero is a platform that offers a range of tools and services to help vacation rental owners manage their properties and bookings.
HomHero.history: HomHero (Homhero) is an Australian SaaS platform providing property management and channel management tools for the holiday rental industry, based on the Gold Coast, Queensland. It powered over 160 clients representing 17,000+ properties as of September 2025. Revenue was approximately AUD $5.2M with 26 employees.
HomHero.summary_abuse: HomHero has no notable abuse signal. It is a specialized property management platform for the Australian vacation rental industry with no documented fraudulent deployment patterns.

Honeybadger.description: Honeybadger provides exception and uptime monitoring to keep your web apps error-free.
Honeybadger.history: Honeybadger was founded in October 2012 by Joshua Wood and Benjamin Curtis as a bootstrapped exception and uptime monitoring service built on Ruby on Rails, targeting Rails and web developers.
Honeybadger.summary_abuse: Honeybadger is a developer-focused exception monitoring tool with no notable abuse signal. It has no consumer-facing content surface and no documented abuse patterns.

Hono.description: Hono is a small, simple, and ultrafast web framework for the Edge.
Hono.history: Hono is an ultrafast TypeScript web framework built on Web Standards, created by Yusuke Wada and first released in 2021. It was designed for Cloudflare Workers and edge runtimes but now supports Node.js, Deno, Bun, and other runtimes. It has grown rapidly in popularity in the edge computing ecosystem.
Hono.summary_abuse: Hono is an open-source web framework library. It has no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal.

hoolah.description: hoolah is Asia's omni-channel buy now pay later platform.
hoolah.history: hoolah was founded in 2018 in Singapore as Asia's omni-channel buy now pay later platform. In November 2021, ShopBack acquired hoolah to strengthen its BNPL offerings across Asia-Pacific, and hoolah's services were rebranded as ShopBack PayLater.
hoolah.summary_abuse: hoolah (now ShopBack PayLater) is a BNPL platform serving legitimate retail merchants in Asia-Pacific. BNPL platforms carry some fraud risk through consumer account fraud and synthetic identity abuse, but hoolah's merchant-focused model and parent company ShopBack's established compliance posture limit notable abuse exposure.

Hoory.description: Hoory is a Conversational AI platform that automates customer support.
Hoory.history: Hoory is a conversational AI customer support platform based in Armenia (Yerevan) with a US office in Glendale, California; it was launched around 2021.
Hoory.summary_abuse: Hoory is a customer support chatbot platform with no notable abuse signal. Its AI automation features are oriented toward business customer service rather than consumer content hosting.

Hoowla.description: Hoowla is a case management software designed to organize, track, and manage legal workflows and client information for law firms.
Hoowla.history: Hoowla is a UK-based cloud legal case management platform serving over 350 UK law firms, specializing in conveyancing and property law; no notable acquisitions or ownership changes found.
Hoowla.summary_abuse: Hoowla is a legal case management platform for UK solicitors. It has no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal.

Horoshop.description: Horoshop is an all-in-one platform designed to support launching and managing online stores tailored for businesses in  Ukraine.
Horoshop.history: Horoshop was founded in 2014 in Kyiv, Ukraine as a SaaS ecommerce platform for Ukrainian online retailers, providing store builder, CMS, and payment/delivery integrations.
Horoshop.summary_abuse: Horoshop is a Ukrainian ecommerce SaaS for online retailers with no notable international abuse signal. Ecommerce platforms can host fake storefronts, but no specific documented campaigns target Horoshop.

Hostens.description: Hostens is a web hosting company specialising in hosting services, virtual private server hosting, and the domain name or transition.
Hostens.history: Hostens was established in 2003 as a division of Lithuanian company Interneto Vizija. It grew to serve over 100,000 clients across Europe, operating its own Tier III data center in Vilnius. It offers shared hosting, VPS, and domain services at competitive prices.
Hostens.summary_abuse: Hostens carries a minor abuse risk typical of affordable shared web hosting providers. Budget hosting is occasionally used to host phishing or spam sites. No major documented incidents are specific to Hostens, and its primary market is legitimate European small businesses.

HostEurope.description: HostEurope is a European website hosting, email and domain name registrar company headquartered Hayes, West London.
HostEurope.history: Host Europe was founded in 1997 in Cologne, Germany as a web hosting and domain registrar. It grew to become Europe's largest independent web hosting group before being acquired by private equity firm Cinven for £438 million in 2013, then by GoDaddy for €1.69 billion in 2017.
HostEurope.summary_abuse: HostEurope is a major European web hosting provider now under GoDaddy. As a general-purpose hosting provider with shared hosting tiers, some proportion of hosted sites may be abusive. GoDaddy/HostEurope has an abuse reporting channel, but large hosting platforms often have mixed track records on rapid takedowns.

Hostgator.description: HostGator is a Houston-based provider of shared, reseller, virtual private server, and dedicated web hosting with an additional presence in Austin, Texas.
Hostgator.history: HostGator was founded in October 2002 by Brent Oxley at Florida Atlantic University and grew into one of the US's largest shared hosting providers. Endurance International Group (EIG) acquired it in July 2012 for $299.8 million. EIG merged with Web.com in 2021 to form Newfold Digital, the current parent.
Hostgator.summary_abuse: HostGator is a mainstream shared hosting provider owned by Newfold Digital. Like other large shared hosting platforms, a small proportion of hosted sites are used for phishing, malware, and spam. HostGator publishes an AUP and abuse contact, and Newfold Digital has automated abuse detection, but the platform's low-cost shared hosting has historically attracted some abusive signups.

Hosting Ukraine.description: Hosting Ukraine is a web hosting provider and internet domain registrar.
Hosting Ukraine.history: Hosting Ukraine (ukraine.com.ua) is a Ukrainian web hosting provider and domain registrar; no notable ownership changes or acquisitions found.
Hosting Ukraine.summary_abuse: Hosting Ukraine is a regional web hosting provider. As a shared hosting platform, some proportion of hosted sites could be abusive, but no specific documented abuse campaigns are attributed to the platform specifically.

Hostinger.description: Hostinger is an employee-owned Web hosting provider and internet domain registrar.
Hostinger.history: Hostinger was founded in 2004 in Kaunas, Lithuania as Hosting Media. In 2007 the company launched free hosting service 000webhost. The company rebranded to Hostinger in 2011. It has expanded globally with subsidiaries in Indonesia, Brazil, and other markets, and launched the AI Website Builder in 2023. As of recent years the company reported 29 million customers and $100M+ in revenue.
Hostinger.summary_abuse: Hostinger is a major global web hosting provider serving 29 million customers. The very low entry price points and high volume of hosted sites make it a common platform for spam operators and phishing campaigns. Hostinger publishes an AUP and processes abuse reports, but due to the scale and low-cost nature of its offerings, abuse incidents occur regularly on the platform.

Hostinger CDN.history: Hostinger was founded in 2004 in Kaunas, Lithuania as Hosting Media, rebranding to Hostinger in 2011. Its CDN is a component of its global hosting infrastructure, which spans ten data centers across eight countries.
Hostinger CDN.summary_abuse: Hostinger CDN is part of a mainstream hosting platform. As with any large CDN/hosting provider, a small proportion of served content may be abusive. Hostinger publishes an AUP and has abuse reporting mechanisms.

Hostinger Horizons.description: Hostinger Horizons is a no-code web app builder designed to simplify web application creation without requiring programming knowledge.
Hostinger Horizons.history: Hostinger Horizons is a no-code web app builder launched by Hostinger around 2024, leveraging AI to generate web applications from user descriptions. It is a product line within Hostinger International, founded in 2004 in Lithuania.
Hostinger Horizons.summary_abuse: Hostinger Horizons is a no-code AI web app builder. Its free tier with hosted deployments could theoretically be used for phishing or scam landing pages, consistent with any low-friction page-builder. No specific documented abuse patterns exist.

Hostinger Website Builder.description: Hostinger Website Builder is a web-based platform that allows users to create and design websites without needing to write code or have extensive technical knowledge.
Hostinger Website Builder.history: Hostinger Website Builder was launched in 2019 as a drag-and-drop no-code builder under the Hostinger hosting brand (founded 2004, Lithuania). In 2023 Hostinger added AI-powered site generation capabilities.
Hostinger Website Builder.summary_abuse: Hostinger Website Builder is a mainstream drag-and-drop site builder with a low-cost entry point. Like other large website builders, a portion of created sites may be used for fake storefronts or phishing landing pages. Hostinger operates an AUP and abuse reporting channel.

Hostiq.description: Hostiq is a web hosting provider and internet domain registrar.
Hostiq.history: HOSTiQ was founded around 2010 in Kharkiv, Ukraine as a web hosting provider and domain registrar with servers in Ukraine, Netherlands, US, and France.
Hostiq.summary_abuse: HOSTiQ is a Ukrainian regional web hosting provider. As a shared hosting platform, a small proportion of hosted sites could be used abusively, but no specific documented abuse campaigns are attributed to the platform.

Hostmeapp.description: Hostmeapp is an restaurant software. Includes reservation, waitlist, guestbook and marketing tools.
Hostmeapp.history: Hostmeapp is a restaurant management SaaS providing reservation, waitlist, guestbook, and marketing tools; minimal public founding history is available.
Hostmeapp.summary_abuse: Hostmeapp is a restaurant reservation and waitlist management tool with no notable abuse signal.

Hostpoint.description: Hostpoint is a Switzerland-based web hosting company.
Hostpoint.history: Hostpoint was founded in spring 2001 by Markus Gebert and Claudius Röllin in Rapperswil-Jona, Switzerland, and has grown into Switzerland's largest web hosting provider and leading domain registrar, remaining independently owned for over 25 years.
Hostpoint.summary_abuse: Hostpoint is Switzerland's largest independent web hosting provider. As a shared hosting platform, a minority of hosted sites could be abusive, but the platform operates under Swiss data protection law and has a strong reputation for responsive support.

Hosttech Website Creator.description: Hosttech Website Creator is a web development tool by the Swiss company Hosttech, providing customizable templates and website management features.
Hosttech Website Creator.history: Hosttech Website Creator is a drag-and-drop website builder offered by hosttech, a Swiss web hosting company with two data centers in Switzerland. It provides 50+ design templates and is bundled with hosttech hosting plans. Data is stored in Switzerland under Swiss and EU privacy law.
Hosttech Website Creator.summary_abuse: Hosttech Website Creator has no notable abuse signal. It is a Swiss-hosted website builder with data localized in Switzerland. No documented fraudulent deployment patterns are associated with it.

Hotaru CMS.history: Hotaru CMS is an open-source PHP-based social content management system built around a plugin architecture, first released around 2009. Development appears to have stalled with no significant updates in many years.
Hotaru CMS.summary_abuse: Hotaru CMS is an abandoned open-source PHP CMS. Outdated PHP installations can be vulnerable to exploitation for spam hosting or malware distribution, but the platform has no specific documented abuse pattern.

Hotbot.description: Hotbot is a platform that enables online management of hotel service sales.
Hotbot.history: Hotbot.ai was founded in 2018 as an AI-powered chatbot and online sales management platform for the hospitality industry.
Hotbot.summary_abuse: Hotbot is a hospitality AI chatbot with no notable abuse signal. It operates as a B2B tool for hotel operations.

HotDoc.description: HotDoc is a platform for booking healthcare appointments, providing patients with access to medical practitioners and clinics.
HotDoc.history: HotDoc was founded in 2012 by Dr. Ben Hurst, John Servinis, Simon Holland, and Tom Spacek in Melbourne, Australia as an online medical appointment booking platform. It has grown into Australia's largest patient engagement platform, used by 1 in 5 Australians.
HotDoc.summary_abuse: HotDoc is an Australian healthcare appointment booking platform with no notable abuse signal. Medical appointment systems handle personal health data but are not typically targeted for fraud schemes.

Hotel Propeller.description: Hotel Propeller is a web design platform that creates responsive websites tailored for hotels.
Hotel Propeller.history: Hotel Propeller is a web design platform that creates responsive websites for hotels; minimal public founding history is available.
Hotel Propeller.summary_abuse: Hotel Propeller is a hotel website builder with no notable abuse signal. It serves the hospitality industry and does not expose consumer-facing arbitrary content hosting.

Hotjar.description: Hotjar is a suite of analytic tools to assist in the gathering of qualitative data, providing feedback through tools such as heatmaps, session recordings, and surveys.
Hotjar.history: Hotjar was founded in 2014 in Malta by David Darmanin and a small team, growing to serve over 1.1 million websites in 180+ countries. In September 2021, Contentsquare acquired Hotjar for an undisclosed sum, combining SMB and enterprise digital analytics capabilities. In 2024, Contentsquare integrated all Hotjar tools into the Contentsquare platform.
Hotjar.summary_abuse: Hotjar records user sessions and captures heatmap data on websites where it is installed. As a behavior analytics tool, it collects sensitive user interaction data including mouse movements, clicks, and form inputs. Privacy advocates have raised concerns about Hotjar capturing personally identifiable information via session recordings, particularly on sites that do not properly configure data masking. Hotjar has implemented GDPR controls including IP anonymization and data masking features. No direct evidence of Hotjar being used maliciously, but its session recording capabilities make it a potential privacy risk if misconfigured.

Hotjar Incoming Feedback.description: Hotjar Incoming Feedback is a widget that sits at the edge of a page.
Hotjar Incoming Feedback.history: Hotjar was founded in 2014 in Malta by David Darmanin and team as an analytics and feedback tool. Its Incoming Feedback widget was a page-edge feedback tool that allowed visitors to rate and annotate pages. Hotjar was acquired by Contentsquare in 2021. In September 2024, the Incoming Feedback feature was migrated into Hotjar Surveys, effectively deprecating it as a standalone widget.
Hotjar Incoming Feedback.summary_abuse: Hotjar Incoming Feedback has low abuse signal. As a session analytics and feedback widget owned by Contentsquare, it collects user interaction data which raises privacy considerations but is not notably exploited for fraud or scams. Hotjar has published GDPR documentation and a DPA. The primary risk is misuse of collected visitor data by site operators rather than direct abuse of the platform.

Hotmart.description: HotMart is a Brazilian-based affiliate marketing system.
Hotmart.history: Hotmart was founded in 2011 in Belo Horizonte, Brazil, by Mateus Bicalho and Jo Costa as a digital products marketplace for creators to sell courses, ebooks, and memberships. It grew to become one of the largest digital commerce platforms in Latin America, expanding internationally. The company has raised significant VC funding and operates across multiple countries.
Hotmart.summary_abuse: Hotmart carries a moderate abuse score because its affiliate marketing and digital products model is commonly exploited. The platform hosts digital courses and ebooks from thousands of independent creators, some of whom sell low-quality or misleading products. Affiliate links generated by the platform are frequently used in spam campaigns and misleading ads. The free creator signup with performance-based fees means low friction for bad actors. Hotmart publishes terms of service and responds to complaints but the volume of creator content makes enforcement uneven.

Housecall Pro.description: Housecall Pro is a platform that enables businesses to schedule, dispatch, invoice, and receive online bookings from customers.
Housecall Pro.history: Housecall Pro was founded in 2013 in San Diego, California, as a field service management SaaS platform for home service businesses. The company raised over $100M in VC funding and grew to serve tens of thousands of service contractors across plumbing, HVAC, electrical, and similar trades.
Housecall Pro.summary_abuse: Housecall Pro has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B SaaS platform for home service businesses, with no consumer-facing content hosting or anonymous account creation. The service is not a known tool for fraud or scams.

Howler.js.description: Howler.js is an audio library with support for the Web Audio API and a fallback mechanism for HTML5 Audio.
Howler.js.history: Howler.js was created by James Simpson and first released in 2013 as an open-source JavaScript audio library. It became the de facto standard for web audio due to its support for the Web Audio API with an HTML5 Audio fallback, making it work reliably across browsers. No major ownership changes or controversies.
Howler.js.summary_abuse: Howler.js has no notable abuse signal. It is a client-side JavaScript audio library with no network-facing surface that scammers would target.

HP Compact Server.history: HP ProLiant Compact Servers (later HPE) are a line of rack-mounted servers developed by Hewlett-Packard since the 1990s. HP split into HP Inc. and Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) in 2015, with the server line moving to HPE. HPE continues to develop ProLiant and related compact server lines for data center and enterprise use.
HP Compact Server.summary_abuse: HP/HPE server hardware is enterprise data-center infrastructure with no meaningful abuse surface. No documented misuse beyond the standard concerns of any physical server hardware. HPE maintains a responsible disclosure program for firmware vulnerabilities.

HP iLO.description: HP iLO is a tool that provides multiple ways to configure, update, monitor, and run servers remotely.
HP iLO.history: HP iLO (Integrated Lights-Out) is a proprietary embedded server management technology developed by Hewlett-Packard, first released around 2001. It enables remote server administration independent of the OS. When HP split in 2015, iLO remained with Hewlett Packard Enterprise. iLO 5 was released in 2017 supporting Gen10 servers, and iLO 6 in 2022 for Gen11. Security vulnerabilities in iLO have periodically attracted attention, including exploits targeting unpatched iLO interfaces exposed to the internet.
HP iLO.summary_abuse: HP iLO is a server remote-management interface. When left exposed on the internet without patching, iLO has been exploited by threat actors including APT groups. Several critical CVEs have targeted iLO (e.g., CVE-2017-12542 allowing authentication bypass). Security researchers strongly advise isolating iLO interfaces from public networks. HPE publishes firmware updates but response time varies. No user-facing abuse surface.

HrFlow.ai.description: HrFlow.ai is an HR data automation API platform.
HrFlow.ai.history: HrFlow.ai was founded in 2020 in Paris, France, as an HR data automation API platform focused on parsing and standardizing HR documents such as resumes and job descriptions using AI. It targets ATS vendors and HR software companies as API customers. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes.
HrFlow.ai.summary_abuse: HrFlow.ai has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B API platform for HR data processing and is not exposed to consumer-facing fraud or scam vectors.

HSTS.description: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) informs browsers that the site should only be accessed using HTTPS.
HSTS.history: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) was standardized as RFC 6797 in November 2012 by the IETF. It was originally proposed by Collin Jackson and Adam Barth in 2009. HSTS preloading (via browser-bundled lists) was later introduced by Google Chrome to extend protection to first visits. Now universally supported by all major browsers and widely deployed by web servers.
HSTS.summary_abuse: HSTS is a browser security standard with no abuse surface. It is a defensive mechanism that forces HTTPS connections, reducing man-in-the-middle attack risk. No documented misuse patterns.

HT Mega.history: HT Mega is a WordPress plugin providing 80+ Elementor widgets and addons, developed by HasThemes, a Bangladeshi WordPress theme and plugin company. Released around 2019, it gained traction as one of many competing Elementor addon packs. HasThemes also produces other WordPress products.
HT Mega.summary_abuse: HT Mega is a WordPress Elementor widget plugin with no significant documented abuse patterns. As a small Bangladeshi WordPress plugin vendor, it lacks formal security programs. Users should keep the plugin updated as Elementor addon plugins are frequent vectors for WordPress site vulnerabilities. No notable CVEs or abuse incidents documented.

html2canvas.history: html2canvas was created by Niklas von Hertzen and first released around 2012. It renders HTML elements to a canvas element by reading the DOM and building a representation of the page using JavaScript, without requiring server-side rendering. It has remained a popular open-source library with no major ownership changes.
html2canvas.summary_abuse: html2canvas is a client-side JavaScript library with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signal.

HTML5 Media.description: HTML5 Media enables video and audio tags in all major browsers.
HTML5 Media.history: HTML5 Media refers to the native audio and video elements introduced in the HTML5 specification. Proposed by the WHATWG around 2004-2007 and formally standardized in the W3C HTML5 spec finalized in 2014. The native media elements replaced the need for Flash and other plugins for browser-based media playback. Now universally supported in all modern browsers and maintained as a living standard by WHATWG.
HTML5 Media.summary_abuse: HTML5 Media is a browser standard with no abuse surface of its own. The video and audio elements are foundational web technology. No documented misuse patterns beyond general web content concerns.

Htmx.description: Htmx is a JavaScript library for performing AJAX requests, triggering CSS transitions, and invoking WebSocket and server-sent events directly from HTML elements.
Htmx.history: Htmx was created by Carson Gross and first released in 2020 as a successor to intercooler.js, which he had developed since 2013. It allows HTML to make AJAX requests, CSS transitions, and WebSocket connections without writing JavaScript. Htmx gained significant traction in the developer community as an alternative to heavy JavaScript frameworks, inspired by HATEOS principles.
Htmx.summary_abuse: Htmx has no notable abuse signal. It is a client-side JavaScript library that extends HTML attributes, with no network service or hosted content that scammers would target.

HTTP/2.description: HTTP/2 (originally named HTTP/2.0) is a major revision of the HTTP network protocol used by the World Wide Web.
HTTP/2.history: HTTP/2 was standardized as RFC 7540 in May 2015 by the IETF HTTPbis working group, based on Google's SPDY protocol. It introduced multiplexing, header compression (HPACK), and server push over a single TCP connection. HTTP/2 is now supported by all major web servers (nginx, Apache, IIS) and CDNs. Updated by RFC 9113 in 2022 to obsolete the original RFC.
HTTP/2.summary_abuse: HTTP/2 is an IETF transport protocol with no inherent abuse surface. It is a foundational web standard. No documented protocol-level misuse patterns unique to HTTP/2 beyond general HTTPS traffic concerns.

HTTP/3.description: HTTP/3 is the third major version of the Hypertext Transfer Protocol used to exchange information on the World Wide Web.
HTTP/3.history: HTTP/3 was standardized as RFC 9114 in June 2022 by the IETF. It is built on QUIC (RFC 9000), a UDP-based transport protocol originally developed by Google as QUIC (Quick UDP Internet Connections). HTTP/3 eliminates head-of-line blocking present in HTTP/2's TCP-based multiplexing. Cloudflare, Google, and Meta were early adopters. Major web servers and CDNs now support HTTP/3 with QUIC.
HTTP/3.summary_abuse: HTTP/3 is an IETF transport protocol with no inherent abuse surface. Being UDP-based, it can be harder for firewalls to inspect but is not specifically associated with abuse. No documented protocol-level misuse patterns unique to HTTP/3.

Hub Platform.description: Hub Platform is a suite of data-driven marketing and customer experience management solutions.
Hub Platform.history: Hub Platform is a marketing and customer experience management suite; minimal public founding history available.
Hub Platform.summary_abuse: Hub Platform is a marketing tools suite with no notable abuse signal.

Hubalz.history: Hubalz is an all-in-one analytics and marketing tool; minimal public founding history available.
Hubalz.summary_abuse: Hubalz is a small analytics and marketing tool with no notable abuse signal.

Hubb.description: Hubb is an all-in-one integrated system designed for churches, enabling website content management without the need for technical expertise.

Hubb.summary_abuse: Hubb (hubb.church) is a church management and website CMS with no notable abuse signal.

Huberway.description: Huberway is a content management system, based on PHP and JavaScript, used to create advanced sales portals oriented towards industrialization 4.0.
Huberway.history: Huberway is a PHP/JavaScript-based CMS for sales portals; minimal public founding history available.
Huberway.summary_abuse: Huberway is a niche CMS for sales portals with no notable abuse signal.

Huberway Analytics.description: Huberway Analytics is a free web analytics service that tracks and reports website traffic.
Huberway Analytics.history: Huberway Analytics is a free web analytics service provided by Huberway; minimal public founding history available.
Huberway Analytics.summary_abuse: Huberway Analytics is a free web analytics tool with no notable abuse signal.

Hubilo.description: Hubilo is a platform offering branded interactive experiences, AI-driven content repurposing, and behavioral analytics to maximize webinar ROI.
Hubilo.history: Hubilo was founded in 2015 in India and originally operated as a physical event management company before pivoting to virtual and hybrid event management SaaS in 2020 during the COVID-19 pandemic. The company is VC-backed and later relocated its headquarters to San Francisco. It rebranded from an event logistics company to a webinar and virtual event platform.
Hubilo.summary_abuse: Hubilo has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B virtual event and webinar platform with enterprise-only pricing that requires vendor engagement, providing little opportunity for anonymous abuse.

HubSpot.description: HubSpot is a marketing and sales software that helps companies attract visitors, convert leads, and close customers.
HubSpot.history: HubSpot was founded in 2006 by Brian Halligan and Dharmesh Shah at MIT in Cambridge, Massachusetts, pioneering the concept of inbound marketing. The company went public on the NYSE in 2014 under ticker HUBS at $25/share, raising over $140M. It has grown through acquisitions including Oneforty (2010), Kemvi, PieSync, Clearbit, The Hustle, and Frame AI. HubSpot now offers a full CRM platform covering marketing, sales, service, CMS, and operations.
HubSpot.summary_abuse: HubSpot is a major marketing and CRM platform occasionally used to send marketing spam via its email marketing tools, and its forms and landing pages have been used in phishing campaigns. HubSpot actively monitors for abuse and has terms of service prohibiting spam. As a large platform with millions of users, some misuse is inevitable, but HubSpot has a dedicated abuse team and responds to reports. Phishing pages hosted on hubspot.com or hubspotpagebuilder.com subdomains are occasionally reported.

HubSpot Analytics.description: HubSpot is a marketing and sales software that helps companies attract visitors, convert leads, and close customers.
HubSpot Analytics.history: HubSpot was founded in 2006 by Brian Halligan and Dharmesh Shah at MIT, pioneering the concept of inbound marketing. The company went public on the NYSE in 2014. HubSpot Analytics is the tracking and reporting component of HubSpot's marketing platform, collecting website visitor and campaign data for customers.
HubSpot Analytics.summary_abuse: HubSpot Analytics has low abuse signal. As a marketing analytics tracking script deployed on customer websites, it collects visitor data for legitimate marketing purposes. HubSpot is a publicly traded company with a robust trust portal, SOC 2 certification, GDPR DPA, and bug bounty program. The primary privacy concern is that the tracking script collects behavioral data, but this is consistent with standard marketing analytics and HubSpot has a visible AUP and compliance program.

HubSpot Chat.description: HubSpot Chat is a tool where you can view, manage, and reply to incoming messages from multiple channels.
HubSpot Chat.history: HubSpot Chat is the live chat and chatbot feature within HubSpot's free CRM suite. It was developed as part of HubSpot's expansion from a marketing automation platform into a full CRM. The feature integrates with HubSpot's shared inbox, connecting website chat, WhatsApp, and Facebook Messenger. It was built and is maintained by HubSpot, a public company founded in 2006.
HubSpot Chat.summary_abuse: HubSpot Chat embeds a live chat widget on websites and collects visitor interaction data stored in HubSpot CRM. As part of the broader HubSpot platform, it inherits the same privacy data collection characteristics. No specific abuse patterns documented for the chat widget itself beyond the general HubSpot platform risks.

HubSpot CMS Hub.description: CMS Hub is a content management platform by HubSpot for marketers to manage, optimize, and track content performance on websites, blogs, and landing pages.
HubSpot CMS Hub.history: HubSpot CMS Hub is the website and content management component of HubSpot's platform, launched as an integrated CMS tightly coupled with HubSpot's CRM. It was rebranded as Content Hub around 2022-2023 to reflect broader content management capabilities beyond website building. Built and maintained by HubSpot, a public company founded 2006 in Cambridge, Massachusetts.
HubSpot CMS Hub.summary_abuse: HubSpot CMS Hub pages have occasionally been used to host phishing landing pages that impersonate legitimate brands, leveraging the hubspotpagebuilder.com domain or custom domains. As with all website builders, the free tier with custom domain support creates some abuse risk. HubSpot actively takes down reported phishing content and maintains an abuse reporting mechanism.

HubSpot Cookie Policy Banner.description: HubSpot Cookie Policy banner is a cookie compliance functionality from HubSpot.
HubSpot Cookie Policy Banner.history: HubSpot Cookie Policy Banner is a cookie consent and compliance feature built into the HubSpot CMS and marketing platform, introduced to help customers comply with GDPR and ePrivacy regulations. HubSpot was founded in 2006 and went public in 2014. The cookie banner functionality has been part of the platform since the introduction of GDPR compliance features around 2018.
HubSpot Cookie Policy Banner.summary_abuse: HubSpot Cookie Policy Banner has no notable abuse signal. It is a compliance feature of HubSpot's platform designed to obtain and manage cookie consent. As a feature from a large publicly traded company with a visible compliance and security program, it is not a known vector for fraud or scams.

HubSpot WordPress plugin.description: HubSpot is a platform with all the tools and integrations you need for marketing, sales, and customer service. HubSpot WordPress plugin allows you to manage contacts (CRM), engage visitors with live chat and chatbots, add beautiful forms to pages, create engaging email marketing campaigns, and more.
HubSpot WordPress plugin.history: The HubSpot WordPress plugin (originally called Leadin) was created by HubSpot and published on the WordPress plugin repository. HubSpot was founded in 2006 by Brian Halligan and Dharmesh Shah and went public in 2014. The WordPress plugin serves as an integration point that connects WordPress sites to HubSpot's CRM, forms, live chat, and analytics tools.
HubSpot WordPress plugin.summary_abuse: HubSpot WordPress plugin has low abuse signal. It is an official integration plugin from a publicly traded company connecting WordPress to HubSpot CRM services. The plugin embeds HubSpot tracking code, which collects visitor data, but this is consistent with its stated purpose and HubSpot has published GDPR DPA, SOC 2 certification, and an AUP.

Hubtiger.description: Hubtiger offers rental and workshop management software solutions to help businesses elevate their operations and efficiency.
Hubtiger.history: Hubtiger was launched in 2018 in Johannesburg, South Africa by Stuart Blyth as rental and workshop management software for bicycle shops. The platform is used by around 300 stores across 15 countries.
Hubtiger.summary_abuse: Hubtiger is a niche workshop management SaaS for bike shops with no notable abuse signal.

Huckabuy.description: Huckabuy is a software product utilising Google's dynamic rendering and structured data initiatives to enhance organic channel growth.

Hugo.description: Hugo is an open-source static site generator written in Go.

HulkApps Form Builder.history: HulkApps Form Builder is one of 18+ Shopify apps developed by HulkApps, a Chicago-headquartered Shopify expert company founded in 2012 that started with Shopify themes before expanding into the app ecosystem. HulkApps Form Builder.summary_abuse: HulkApps Form Builder is a Shopify app for creating custom forms. It operates within Shopify's ecosystem with no standalone hosting surface. No documented abuse incidents are associated with this specific app or HulkApps as a developer.

HulkApps Infinite Product Options.description: HulkApps Infinite Product Options is a unlimited custom options for products. Display variant options as buttons, color and image swatches, and more.
HulkApps Infinite Product Options.history: HulkApps Infinite Product Options is a Shopify app allowing merchants to add unlimited custom product options and variants, developed by HulkApps, a Chicago-based Shopify expert agency founded in 2012. The app is one of HulkApps' most popular offerings in the Shopify App Store.
HulkApps Infinite Product Options.summary_abuse: HulkApps Infinite Product Options is a Shopify product customization app with no standalone hosting or consumer-facing surface. No documented abuse incidents are associated with this app.

HulkApps Product Reviews.description: HulkApps Product Reviews is a customer product reviews app for building social proof for store.
HulkApps Product Reviews.history: HulkApps Product Reviews is a customer review collection and display app for Shopify stores, developed by HulkApps, a Chicago-based Shopify expert agency founded in 2012. It allows merchants to collect, moderate, and display social proof on product pages.

Human Presence.description: Human Presence is a bot detection and spam protection software for WordPress and Shopify.

It integrates with popular WordPress form plugins including Gravity Forms, WPForms, and Contact Form 7. The company is small and independent, focused on the WordPress ecosystem.

The behavioral fingerprinting approach it uses collects interaction data from site visitors, which is a privacy consideration, though data is used only for bot detection. No documented abuse of the service itself.

Humblytics.description: Humblytics is an analytics tool that simplifies tracking custom events without coding. It monitors real-time metrics, optimises conversion rates, and provides valuable insights.

Humblytics.summary_abuse: Humblytics has no notable abuse signal. It is a privacy-first analytics platform that explicitly avoids PII collection. No documented fraudulent deployment patterns are associated with it.

Humm.description: Humm (formerly Flexigroup) is a buy now pay later service operating in Australia.
Humm.history: Humm is an Australian buy now pay later (BNPL) service that was a major player in the Australian market alongside Afterpay and Zip. In June 2025, the humm Classic BNPL product was retired. The company faced an interim stop order from ASIC in 2023 following a review of its lending practices. The business transitioned to a new product model from June 10, 2025.
Humm.summary_abuse: Humm received an interim stop order from ASIC in 2023 during a review of its buy now pay later lending practices, indicating regulatory concern about its consumer lending approach. BNPL services generally carry risk of enabling consumer debt problems and have been scrutinized for insufficient creditworthiness assessments. The Australian government enacted the Treasury Laws Amendment (Responsible Buy Now Pay Later) Bill 2024 to address industry-wide concerns.

Hummerce.description: Hummerce is a B2C/B2B ecommerce platform designed to support online sales with a stable and scalable foundation.
Hummerce.history: Hummerce is a B2C/B2B ecommerce platform providing stable and scalable online sales infrastructure. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.

It was built to address the gap between monitoring tools and communication tools in the status page market. It competes with Atlassian Statuspage and Status.io and is notable for a developer-centric approach with full Terraform support and usage-based pricing.
Hund.io.summary_abuse: Hund.io is a status page and monitoring platform for engineering teams with no meaningful consumer-facing attack surface. Its services are used by legitimate companies to communicate system status. No documented abuse history.

HurryTimer.description: HurryTimer is a cutting-edge WordPress plugin that enables users to create customizable, scheduled countdown timers to further engage visitors and increase conversions.
HurryTimer.history: HurryTimer is a WordPress plugin for creating countdown timers, available on the WordPress plugin repository. It offers a free version plus premium add-ons. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
HurryTimer.summary_abuse: HurryTimer is a countdown timer WordPress plugin. Like other urgency/scarcity tools, it can be used for dark-pattern fake countdown timers on manipulative sales pages, but the plugin itself has no documented abuse at the platform level.

Hushly.description: Hushly is an all-in-one B2B marketing software platform.
Hushly.history: Hushly was founded around 2013 as a B2B content engagement and lead conversion platform leveraging AI-powered personalization. The company is headquartered in the US and focuses on helping B2B marketers improve content ROI through AI-driven recommendations and form optimization. No notable ownership changes or controversies.
Hushly.summary_abuse: Hushly is a B2B marketing platform with no notable abuse signals. The platform collects lead data including email addresses and form submissions on behalf of its customers, which raises standard data privacy considerations but no documented misuse. The vendor publishes a privacy policy and supports GDPR compliance for its customers.

Hydra-Shield.description: Hydra-Shield is an anti-DDoS protection reverse proxy that filters and mitigates malicious traffic to safeguard servers from distributed denial-of-service attacks.
Hydra-Shield.history: Hydra-Shield is a French anti-DDoS protection service operating as a reverse proxy that filters and mitigates malicious traffic. Based in Val d'Oight, France, the service was established around 2020 and targets small to mid-sized websites needing Layer 7 DDoS protection. The platform offers web protection and IP tunnel features for servers. It is a small independent French cybersecurity startup with limited public presence beyond its website and Trustpilot reviews. The service competes with larger players like Cloudflare and OVH in the French market by offering localized support.
Hydra-Shield.summary_abuse: No abuse concerns identified. Hydra-Shield is a legitimate anti-DDoS service. As a reverse proxy/infrastructure provider, it handles traffic on behalf of customers, which carries standard risks for any CDN or security proxy (potential for misuse by customers), but no documented incidents of abuse by the operator itself. Being a small French startup, it lacks formal security certifications but is subject to EU/French law.

Hydrogen.description: Hydrogen is a front-end web development framework used for building Shopify custom storefronts.
Hydrogen.history: Hydrogen is Shopify's open-source React-based headless commerce framework, initially developed in 2021 and first publicly released at Shopify Unite 2022. It enables developers to build custom storefronts using Shopify's Storefront API with React Server Components, optimized caching, and built-in commerce primitives. The MIT-licensed GitHub repository has 2,691 commits and 449 releases, with the latest release in April 2026 confirming active maintenance. Shopify (TSX/NYSE: SHOP) is a publicly traded Canadian company. Hydrogen is designed to deploy on Oxygen (Shopify's hosting) or any Node.js-compatible environment. The companion library @shopify/hydrogen-react can be used framework-agnostically.
Hydrogen.summary_abuse: No abuse concerns. Hydrogen is an official open-source framework maintained by Shopify, a publicly listed and reputable company. MIT license ensures full code transparency and auditability. No reported security vulnerabilities or malicious behavior. Shopify maintains a responsible disclosure program and bug bounty. The framework is strictly a build tool with no runtime data collection by itself.

HypeLab.description: HypeLab is a platform that supports audience development by enhancing visibility, fostering recognition, and helping establish a loyal community.
HypeLab.history: HypeLab was founded in 2022 in San Francisco by Edward Weng (ex-Google AdMob PM) and Joseph Kim. It raised $4M in seed funding led by Shima Capital and Makers Fund to build a Web3 crypto ad network using on-chain wallet-aware targeting.
HypeLab.summary_abuse: HypeLab is a crypto Web3 advertising network. Crypto advertising networks have been associated with the distribution of scam ICO, rug-pull, and phishing ads. The platform's user tracking via on-chain wallet data raises privacy concerns. Legitimate Web3 projects are customers, but the crypto ad sector broadly has a higher-than-average ratio of fraudulent advertising campaigns.

Hypercloudhost.description: Hypercloudhost is a hosting service solution in Indonesia offering anti-DDoS protection and high-performance cloud hosting.
Hypercloudhost.history: Hypercloudhost is an Indonesian web hosting provider with anti-DDoS protection; minimal public founding history available.
Hypercloudhost.summary_abuse: Hypercloudhost is a small Indonesian hosting provider. As a shared hosting platform, some portion of hosted sites could be used abusively, but no specific documented campaigns are attributed to it.

Hypercorn.description: Hypercorn is an ASGI web server that supports HTTP/1, HTTP/2, WebSockets, ASGI/2, and ASGI/3 specifications, and can utilize asyncio, uvloop, or trio for asynchronous operations.
Hypercorn.history: Hypercorn is an open-source Python ASGI web server created by Philip Jones, first released around 2018. It supports HTTP/1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and WebSockets, and is designed for Python async web frameworks.
Hypercorn.summary_abuse: Hypercorn is a Python ASGI web server with no abuse signal. It is a developer infrastructure tool installed on private servers with no consumer-facing hosted service component.

Hyperia.description: Hyperia is a tool designed for lead generation and affiliate marketing.

Hyperlane.description: Hyperlane is a hosting platform for WordPress and Craft CMS websites.

It was created circa 2024 and positioned as a high-performance alternative to heavier Rust web frameworks. The project has received attention on developer community blogs but has a very small user base.
Hyperlane.summary_abuse: Hyperlane has no notable abuse signal. It is a backend HTTP library for Rust developers with no consumer-facing surface.

Hyperspeed.description: Hyperspeed is the most advanced speed booster for Shopify.
Hyperspeed.history: Hyperspeed is a Shopify app focused on storefront performance optimization (speed booster). No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Hyperspeed.summary_abuse: Hyperspeed is a Shopify performance app with no documented abuse signal. Its function is purely technical optimization with no consumer-facing content hosting.

Hyros.description: Hyros is a marketing analytics and optimisation platform.
Hyros.history: Hyros was founded in 2019 by Alex Becker in Austin, Texas as an AI-powered ad tracking and attribution platform targeting digital marketers and SaaS businesses. In December 2022, it was acquired by Banzai International for $110 million. The platform uses server-side tracking and patented AI to provide attribution data independent of ad platform pixels.
Hyros.summary_abuse: Hyros is an ad tracking platform with low direct abuse risk. It collects first-party conversion data on behalf of its customers and does not operate consumer-facing surfaces that could be exploited. As a server-side tracking tool, it does not inject client-side scripts that are commonly abused. No documented abuse incidents or security research reports against the platform.

Hyva Themes.description: Hyva Themes is a performance-optimised theme for Magento 2 which eliminated the third-party libraries and having only two dependencies Alpine.js and Tailwind CSS.
Hyva Themes.history: Hyvä Themes was created by Dutch developer Willem Wigman and launched in February 2021 as a high-performance Magento 2 frontend framework built with Tailwind CSS and Alpine.js, replacing Magento's slow RequireJS/Knockout frontend. Since November 2025 the theme has been released as fully free and open-source under OSL 3.0 and AFL 3.0 licenses.
Hyva Themes.summary_abuse: Hyvä Themes is an open-source Magento 2 theme with no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted frontend library with no consumer-facing surface for exploitation.
